the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package csi\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"io\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"net\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n27: \\n28: \\tcsipbv1 \\\"github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/lib/go/csi\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n33: \\tapi \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n35: \\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume\\\"\\n39: \\tvolumetypes \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util/types\\\"\\n40: )\\n41: \\n42: type csiClient interface {\\n43: \\tNodeGetInfo(ctx context.Context) (\\n44: \\t\\tnodeID string,\\n45: \\t\\tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\\n46: \\t\\taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\\n47: \\t\\terr error)\\n48: \\n49: \\t// The caller is responsible for checking whether the driver supports\\n50: \\t// applying FSGroup by calling NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup().\\n51: \\t// If the driver does not, fsGroup must be set to nil.\\n52: \\tNodePublishVolume(\\n53: \\t\\tctx context.Context,\\n54: \\t\\tvolumeid string,\\n55: \\t\\treadOnly bool,\\n56: \\t\\tstagingTargetPath string,\\n57: \\t\\ttargetPath string,\\n58: \\t\\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\\n59: \\t\\tpublishContext map[string]string,\\n60: \\t\\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\\n61: \\t\\tsecrets map[string]string,\\n62: \\t\\tfsType string,\\n63: \\t\\tmountOptions []string,\\n64: \\t\\tfsGroup *int64,\\n65: \\t) error\\n66: \\n67: \\tNodeExpandVolume(ctx context.Context, rsOpts csiResizeOptions) (resource.Quantity, error)\\n68: \\tNodeUnpublishVolume(\\n69: \\t\\tctx context.Context,\\n70: \\t\\tvolID string,\\n71: \\t\\ttargetPath string,\\n72: \\t) error\\n73: \\n74: \\t// The caller is responsible for checking whether the driver supports\\n75: \\t// applying FSGroup by calling NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup().\\n76: \\t// If the driver does not, fsGroup must be set to nil.\\n77: \\tNodeStageVolume(ctx context.Context,\\n78: \\t\\tvolID string,\\n79: \\t\\tpublishVolumeInfo map[string]string,\\n80: \\t\\tstagingTargetPath string,\\n81: \\t\\tfsType string,\\n82: \\t\\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\\n83: \\t\\tsecrets map[string]string,\\n84: \\t\\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\\n85: \\t\\tmountOptions []string,\\n86: \\t\\tfsGroup *int64,\\n87: \\t) error\\n88: \\n89: \\tNodeGetVolumeStats(\\n90: \\t\\tctx context.Context,\\n91: \\t\\tvolID string,\\n92: \\t\\ttargetPath string,\\n93: \\t) (*volume.Metrics, error)\\n94: \\tNodeUnstageVolume(ctx context.Context, volID, stagingTargetPath string) error\\n95: \\tNodeSupportsStageUnstage(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\\n96: \\tNodeSupportsNodeExpand(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\\n97: \\tNodeSupportsVolumeStats(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\\n98: \\tNodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\\n99: \\tNodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\\n100: }\\n101: \\n102: // Strongly typed address\\n103: type csiAddr string\\n104: \\n105: // Strongly typed driver name\\n106: type csiDriverName string\\n107: \\n108: // csiClient encapsulates all csi-plugin methods\\n109: type csiDriverClient struct {\\n110: \\tdriverName          csiDriverName\\n111: \\taddr                csiAddr\\n112: \\tmetricsManager      *MetricsManager\\n113: \\tnodeV1ClientCreator nodeV1ClientCreator\\n114: }\\n115: \\n116: type csiResizeOptions struct {\\n117: \\tvolumeID          string\\n118: \\tvolumePath        string\\n119: \\tstagingTargetPath string\\n120: \\tfsType            string\\n121: \\taccessMode        api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\n122: \\tnewSize           resource.Quantity\\n123: \\tmountOptions      []string\\n124: \\tsecrets           map[string]string\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: var _ csiClient = &csiDriverClient{}\\n128: \\n129: type nodeV1ClientCreator func(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (\\n130: \\tnodeClient csipbv1.NodeClient,\\n131: \\tcloser io.Closer,\\n132: \\terr error,\\n133: )\\n134: \\n135: type nodeV1AccessModeMapper func(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode\\n136: \\n137: // newV1NodeClient creates a new NodeClient with the internally used gRPC\\n138: // connection set up. It also returns a closer which must be called to close\\n139: // the gRPC connection when the NodeClient is not used anymore.\\n140: // This is the default implementation for the nodeV1ClientCreator, used in\\n141: // newCsiDriverClient.\\n142: func newV1NodeClient(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (nodeClient csipbv1.NodeClient, closer io.Closer, err error) {\\n143: \\tvar conn *grpc.ClientConn\\n144: \\tconn, err = newGrpcConn(addr, metricsManager)\\n145: \\tif err != nil {\\n146: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n147: \\t}\\n148: \\n149: \\tnodeClient = csipbv1.NewNodeClient(conn)\\n150: \\treturn nodeClient, conn, nil\\n151: }\\n152: \\n153: func newCsiDriverClient(driverName csiDriverName) (*csiDriverClient, error) {\\n154: \\tif driverName == \\\"\\\" {\\n155: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"driver name is empty\\\")\\n156: \\t}\\n157: \\n158: \\texistingDriver, driverExists := csiDrivers.Get(string(driverName))\\n159: \\tif !driverExists {\\n160: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"driver name %s not found in the list of registered CSI drivers\\\", driverName)\\n161: \\t}\\n162: \\n163: \\tnodeV1ClientCreator := newV1NodeClient\\n164: \\treturn &csiDriverClient{\\n165: \\t\\tdriverName:          driverName,\\n166: \\t\\taddr:                csiAddr(existingDriver.endpoint),\\n167: \\t\\tnodeV1ClientCreator: nodeV1ClientCreator,\\n168: \\t\\tmetricsManager:      NewCSIMetricsManager(string(driverName)),\\n169: \\t}, nil\\n170: }\\n171: \\n172: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeGetInfo(ctx context.Context) (\\n173: \\tnodeID string,\\n174: \\tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\\n175: \\taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\\n176: \\terr error) {\\n177: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodeGetInfo rpc\\\"))\\n178: \\n179: \\tvar getNodeInfoError error\\n180: \\tnodeID, maxVolumePerNode, accessibleTopology, getNodeInfoError = c.nodeGetInfoV1(ctx)\\n181: \\tif getNodeInfoError != nil {\\n182: \\t\\tklog.InfoS(\\\"Error calling CSI NodeGetInfo()\\\", \\\"err\\\", getNodeInfoError.Error())\\n183: \\t}\\n184: \\treturn nodeID, maxVolumePerNode, accessibleTopology, getNodeInfoError\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeGetInfoV1(ctx context.Context) (\\n188: \\tnodeID string,\\n189: \\tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\\n190: \\taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\\n191: \\terr error) {\\n192: \\n193: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n194: \\tif err != nil {\\n195: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", 0, nil, err\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n198: \\n199: \\tres, err := nodeClient.NodeGetInfo(ctx, &csipbv1.NodeGetInfoRequest{})\\n200: \\tif err != nil {\\n201: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", 0, nil, err\\n202: \\t}\\n203: \\n204: \\ttopology := res.GetAccessibleTopology()\\n205: \\tif topology != nil {\\n206: \\t\\taccessibleTopology = topology.Segments\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\treturn res.GetNodeId(), res.GetMaxVolumesPerNode(), accessibleTopology, nil\\n209: }\\n210: \\n211: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodePublishVolume(\\n212: \\tctx context.Context,\\n213: \\tvolID string,\\n214: \\treadOnly bool,\\n215: \\tstagingTargetPath string,\\n216: \\ttargetPath string,\\n217: \\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\\n218: \\tpublishContext map[string]string,\\n219: \\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\\n220: \\tsecrets map[string]string,\\n221: \\tfsType string,\\n222: \\tmountOptions []string,\\n223: \\tfsGroup *int64,\\n224: ) error {\\n225: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodePublishVolume rpc\\\"), \\\"volID\\\", volID, \\\"targetPath\\\", targetPath)\\n226: \\tif volID == \\\"\\\" {\\n227: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\tif targetPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n230: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing target path\\\")\\n231: \\t}\\n232: \\n233: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n234: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"failed to call NodePublishVolume. nodeV1ClientCreator is nil\\\")\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\\n238: \\tif err != nil {\\n239: \\t\\treturn err\\n240: \\t}\\n241: \\n242: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n247: \\n248: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodePublishVolumeRequest{\\n249: \\t\\tVolumeId:       volID,\\n250: \\t\\tTargetPath:     targetPath,\\n251: \\t\\tReadonly:       readOnly,\\n252: \\t\\tPublishContext: publishContext,\\n253: \\t\\tVolumeContext:  volumeContext,\\n254: \\t\\tSecrets:        secrets,\\n255: \\t\\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\\n256: \\t\\t\\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\\n257: \\t\\t\\t\\tMode: accessModeMapper(accessMode),\\n258: \\t\\t\\t},\\n259: \\t\\t},\\n260: \\t}\\n261: \\tif stagingTargetPath != \\\"\\\" {\\n262: \\t\\treq.StagingTargetPath = stagingTargetPath\\n263: \\t}\\n264: \\n265: \\tif fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\\n266: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\\n267: \\t\\t\\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\\n268: \\t\\t}\\n269: \\t} else {\\n270: \\t\\tmountVolume := &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\\n271: \\t\\t\\tFsType:     fsType,\\n272: \\t\\t\\tMountFlags: mountOptions,\\n273: \\t\\t}\\n274: \\t\\tif fsGroup != nil {\\n275: \\t\\t\\tmountVolume.VolumeMountGroup = strconv.FormatInt(*fsGroup, 10 /* base */)\\n276: \\t\\t}\\n277: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\\n278: \\t\\t\\tMount: mountVolume,\\n279: \\t\\t}\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\n282: \\t_, err = nodeClient.NodePublishVolume(ctx, req)\\n283: \\tif err != nil && !isFinalError(err) {\\n284: \\t\\treturn volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\\n285: \\t}\\n286: \\treturn err\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeExpandVolume(ctx context.Context, opts csiResizeOptions) (resource.Quantity, error) {\\n290: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, fmt.Errorf(\\\"version of CSI driver does not support volume expansion\\\")\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tif opts.volumeID == \\\"\\\" {\\n295: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\tif opts.volumePath == \\\"\\\" {\\n298: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\\\"missing volume path\\\")\\n299: \\t}\\n300: \\n301: \\tif opts.newSize.Value() < 0 {\\n302: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\\\"size can not be less than 0\\\")\\n303: \\t}\\n304: \\n305: \\taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, err\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n311: \\tif err != nil {\\n312: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, err\\n313: \\t}\\n314: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n315: \\n316: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeExpandVolumeRequest{\\n317: \\t\\tVolumeId:      opts.volumeID,\\n318: \\t\\tVolumePath:    opts.volumePath,\\n319: \\t\\tCapacityRange: &csipbv1.CapacityRange{RequiredBytes: opts.newSize.Value()},\\n320: \\t\\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\\n321: \\t\\t\\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\\n322: \\t\\t\\t\\tMode: accessModeMapper(opts.accessMode),\\n323: \\t\\t\\t},\\n324: \\t\\t},\\n325: \\t\\tSecrets: opts.secrets,\\n326: \\t}\\n327: \\n328: \\t// not all CSI drivers support NodeStageUnstage and hence the StagingTargetPath\\n329: \\t// should only be set when available\\n330: \\tif opts.stagingTargetPath != \\\"\\\" {\\n331: \\t\\treq.StagingTargetPath = opts.stagingTargetPath\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\n334: \\tif opts.fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\\n335: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\\n336: \\t\\t\\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\\n337: \\t\\t}\\n338: \\t} else {\\n339: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\\n340: \\t\\t\\tMount: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\\n341: \\t\\t\\t\\tFsType:     opts.fsType,\\n342: \\t\\t\\t\\tMountFlags: opts.mountOptions,\\n343: \\t\\t\\t},\\n344: \\t\\t}\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\n347: \\tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeExpandVolume(ctx, req)\\n348: \\tif err != nil {\\n349: \\t\\tif !isFinalError(err) {\\n350: \\t\\t\\treturn opts.newSize, volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\\n351: \\t\\t}\\n352: \\t\\treturn opts.newSize, err\\n353: \\t}\\n354: \\n355: \\tupdatedQuantity := resource.NewQuantity(resp.CapacityBytes, resource.BinarySI)\\n356: \\treturn *updatedQuantity, nil\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeUnpublishVolume(ctx context.Context, volID string, targetPath string) error {\\n360: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodeUnpublishVolume rpc\\\"), \\\"volID\\\", volID, \\\"targetPath\\\", targetPath)\\n361: \\tif volID == \\\"\\\" {\\n362: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n363: \\t}\\n364: \\tif targetPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n365: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing target path\\\")\\n366: \\t}\\n367: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n368: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n369: \\t}\\n370: \\n371: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n372: \\tif err != nil {\\n373: \\t\\treturn err\\n374: \\t}\\n375: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n376: \\n377: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeUnpublishVolumeRequest{\\n378: \\t\\tVolumeId:   volID,\\n379: \\t\\tTargetPath: targetPath,\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t_, err = nodeClient.NodeUnpublishVolume(ctx, req)\\n383: \\treturn err\\n384: }\\n385: \\n386: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeStageVolume(ctx context.Context,\\n387: \\tvolID string,\\n388: \\tpublishContext map[string]string,\\n389: \\tstagingTargetPath string,\\n390: \\tfsType string,\\n391: \\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\\n392: \\tsecrets map[string]string,\\n393: \\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\\n394: \\tmountOptions []string,\\n395: \\tfsGroup *int64,\\n396: ) error {\\n397: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodeStageVolume rpc\\\"), \\\"volID\\\", volID, \\\"stagingTargetPath\\\", stagingTargetPath)\\n398: \\tif volID == \\\"\\\" {\\n399: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n400: \\t}\\n401: \\tif stagingTargetPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n402: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing staging target path\\\")\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n405: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n406: \\t}\\n407: \\n408: \\taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\\n409: \\tif err != nil {\\n410: \\t\\treturn err\\n411: \\t}\\n412: \\n413: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n414: \\tif err != nil {\\n415: \\t\\treturn err\\n416: \\t}\\n417: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n418: \\n419: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeStageVolumeRequest{\\n420: \\t\\tVolumeId:          volID,\\n421: \\t\\tPublishContext:    publishContext,\\n422: \\t\\tStagingTargetPath: stagingTargetPath,\\n423: \\t\\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\\n424: \\t\\t\\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\\n425: \\t\\t\\t\\tMode: accessModeMapper(accessMode),\\n426: \\t\\t\\t},\\n427: \\t\\t},\\n428: \\t\\tSecrets:       secrets,\\n429: \\t\\tVolumeContext: volumeContext,\\n430: \\t}\\n431: \\n432: \\tif fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\\n433: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\\n434: \\t\\t\\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\\n435: \\t\\t}\\n436: \\t} else {\\n437: \\t\\tmountVolume := &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\\n438: \\t\\t\\tFsType:     fsType,\\n439: \\t\\t\\tMountFlags: mountOptions,\\n440: \\t\\t}\\n441: \\t\\tif fsGroup != nil {\\n442: \\t\\t\\tmountVolume.VolumeMountGroup = strconv.FormatInt(*fsGroup, 10 /* base */)\\n443: \\t\\t}\\n444: \\t\\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\\n445: \\t\\t\\tMount: mountVolume,\\n446: \\t\\t}\\n447: \\t}\\n448: \\n449: \\t_, err = nodeClient.NodeStageVolume(ctx, req)\\n450: \\tif err != nil && !isFinalError(err) {\\n451: \\t\\treturn volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\\n452: \\t}\\n453: \\treturn err\\n454: }\\n455: \\n456: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeUnstageVolume(ctx context.Context, volID, stagingTargetPath string) error {\\n457: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodeUnstageVolume rpc\\\"), \\\"volID\\\", volID, \\\"stagingTargetPath\\\", stagingTargetPath)\\n458: \\tif volID == \\\"\\\" {\\n459: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n460: \\t}\\n461: \\tif stagingTargetPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n462: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"missing staging target path\\\")\\n463: \\t}\\n464: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n465: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n466: \\t}\\n467: \\n468: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n469: \\tif err != nil {\\n470: \\t\\treturn err\\n471: \\t}\\n472: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n473: \\n474: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeUnstageVolumeRequest{\\n475: \\t\\tVolumeId: NodeSupportsNodeExpand(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n483: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_EXPAND_VOLUME)\\n484: }\\n485: \\n486: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsStageUnstage(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n487: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_STAGE_UNSTAGE_VOLUME)\\n488: }\\n489: \\n490: func (c *csiDriverClient) getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx context.Context) (nodeV1AccessModeMapper, error) {\\n491: \\tsupported, err := c.NodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx)\\n492: \\tif err != nil {\\n493: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n494: \\t}\\n495: \\tif supported {\\n496: \\t\\treturn asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1, nil\\n497: \\t}\\n498: \\treturn asCSIAccessModeV1, nil\\n499: }\\n500: \\n501: func asCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\\n502: \\tswitch am {\\n503: \\tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\\n504: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\\n505: \\tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\\n506: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\\n507: \\tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\\n508: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\\n509: \\t// This mapping exists to enable CSI drivers that lack the\\n510: \\t// SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER capability to work with the\\n511: \\t// ReadWriteOncePod access mode.\\n512: \\tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\\n513: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\\n514: \\t}\\n515: \\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\\n516: }\\n517: \\n518: func asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\\n519: \\tswitch am {\\n520: \\tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\\n521: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\\n522: \\tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\\n523: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\\n524: \\tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\\n525: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\\n526: \\tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\\n527: \\t\\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_SINGLE_WRITER\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\\n530: }\\n531: \\n532: func newGrpcConn(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\\n533: \\tnetwork := \\\"unix\\\"\\n534: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"creating new gRPC connection\\\"), \\\"protocol\\\", network, \\\"endpoint\\\", addr)\\n535: \\n536: \\treturn grpc.Dial(\\n537: \\t\\tstring(addr),\\n538: \\t\\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\\\"localhost\\\"),\\n539: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n540: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, target string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n541: \\t\\t\\treturn (&net.Dialer{}).DialContext(ctx, network, target)\\n542: \\t\\t}),\\n543: \\t\\tgrpc.WithChainUnaryInterceptor(metricsManager.RecordMetricsInterceptor),\\n544: \\t)\\n545: }\\n546: \\n547: // CSI client getter with cache.\\n548: // This provides a method to initialize CSI client with driver name and caches\\n549: // it for later use. When CSI clients have not been discovered yet (e.g.\\n550: // on kubelet restart), client initialization will fail. Users of CSI client (e.g.\\n551: // mounter manager and block mapper) can use this to delay CSI client\\n552: // initialization until needed.\\n553: type csiClientGetter struct {\\n554: \\tsync.RWMutex\\n555: \\tcsiClient  csiClient\\n556: \\tdriverName csiDriverName\\n557: }\\n558: \\n559: func (c *csiClientGetter) Get() (csiClient, error) {\\n560: \\tc.RLock()\\n561: \\tif c.csiClient != nil {\\n562: \\t\\tc.RUnlock()\\n563: \\t\\treturn c.csiClient, nil\\n564: \\t}\\n565: \\tc.RUnlock()\\n566: \\tc.Lock()\\n567: \\tdefer c.Unlock()\\n568: \\t// Double-checking locking criterion.\\n569: \\tif c.csiClient != nil {\\n570: \\t\\treturn c.csiClient, nil\\n571: \\t}\\n572: \\tcsi, err := newCsiDriverClient(c.driverName)\\n573: \\tif err != nil {\\n574: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n575: \\t}\\n576: \\tc.csiClient = csi\\n577: \\treturn c.csiClient, nil\\n578: }\\n579: \\n580: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsVolumeStats(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n581: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_GET_VOLUME_STATS)\\n582: }\\n583: \\n584: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n585: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER)\\n586: }\\n587: \\n588: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeGetVolumeStats(ctx context.Context, volID string, targetPath string) (*volume.Metrics, error) {\\n589: \\tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\\\"calling NodeGetVolumeStats rpc\\\"), \\\"volID\\\", volID, \\\"targetPath\\\", targetPath)\\n590: \\tif volID == \\\"\\\" {\\n591: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"missing volume id\\\")\\n592: \\t}\\n593: \\tif targetPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n594: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"missing target path\\\")\\n595: \\t}\\n596: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n597: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n598: \\t}\\n599: \\n600: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n601: \\tif err != nil {\\n602: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n603: \\t}\\n604: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n605: \\n606: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeGetVolumeStatsRequest{\\n607: \\t\\tVolumeId:   volID,\\n608: \\t\\tVolumePath: targetPath,\\n609: \\t}\\n610: \\n611: \\tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeGetVolumeStats(ctx, req)\\n612: \\tif err != nil {\\n613: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n614: \\t}\\n615: \\n616: \\tmetrics := &volume.Metrics{\\n617: \\t\\tUsed:       resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n618: \\t\\tCapacity:   resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n619: \\t\\tAvailable:  resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n620: \\t\\tInodesUsed: resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n621: \\t\\tInodes:     resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n622: \\t\\tInodesFree: resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\\n623: \\t}\\n624: \\n625: \\tvar isSupportNodeVolumeCondition bool\\n626: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CSIVolumeHealth) {\\n627: \\t\\tisSupportNodeVolumeCondition, err = c.nodeSupportsVolumeCondition(ctx)\\n628: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n629: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n630: \\t\\t}\\n631: \\n632: \\t\\tif isSupportNodeVolumeCondition {\\n633: \\t\\t\\tabnormal, message := resp.VolumeCondition.GetAbnormal(), resp.VolumeCondition.GetMessage()\\n634: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Abnormal, metrics.Message = &abnormal, &message\\n635: \\t\\t}\\n636: \\t}\\n637: \\n638: \\tusages := resp.GetUsage()\\n639: \\t// If the driver does not support volume condition and usages is nil, return an error\\n640: \\tif !isSupportNodeVolumeCondition && usages == nil {\\n641: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get usage from response. usage is nil\\\")\\n642: \\t}\\n643: \\n644: \\tfor _, usage := range usages {\\n645: \\t\\tif usage == nil {\\n646: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n647: \\t\\t}\\n648: \\t\\tunit := usage.GetUnit()\\n649: \\t\\tswitch unit {\\n650: \\t\\tcase csipbv1.VolumeUsage_BYTES:\\n651: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Available = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetAvailable(), resource.BinarySI)\\n652: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Capacity = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetTotal(), resource.BinarySI)\\n653: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Used = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetUsed(), resource.BinarySI)\\n654: \\t\\tcase csipbv1.VolumeUsage_INODES:\\n655: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.InodesFree = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetAvailable(), resource.BinarySI)\\n656: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Inodes = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetTotal(), resource.BinarySI)\\n657: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.InodesUsed = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetUsed(), resource.BinarySI)\\n658: \\t\\tdefault:\\n659: \\t\\t\\tklog.ErrorS(nil, \\\"unknown unit in VolumeUsage\\\", \\\"unit\\\", unit.String())\\n660: \\t\\t}\\n661: \\n662: \\t}\\n663: \\treturn metrics, nil\\n664: }\\n665: \\n666: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeSupportsVolumeCondition(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n667: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_VOLUME_CONDITION)\\n668: }\\n669: \\n670: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n671: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_VOLUME_MOUNT_GROUP)\\n672: }\\n673: \\n674: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeSupportsCapability(ctx context.Context, capabilityType csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_Type) (bool, error) {\\n675: \\tklog.V(4).Info(log(\\\"calling NodeGetCapabilities rpc to determine if the node service has %s capability\\\", capabilityType))\\n676: \\tcapabilities, err := c.nodeGetCapabilities(ctx)\\n677: \\tif err != nil {\\n678: \\t\\treturn false, err\\n679: \\t}\\n680: \\n681: \\tfor _, capability := range capabilities {\\n682: \\t\\tif capability == nil || capability.GetRpc() == nil {\\n683: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n684: \\t\\t}\\n685: \\t\\tif capability.GetRpc().GetType() == capabilityType {\\n686: \\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n687: \\t\\t}\\n688: \\t}\\n689: \\treturn false, nil\\n690: }\\n691: \\n692: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeGetCapabilities(ctx context.Context) ([]*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability, error) {\\n693: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n694: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n695: \\t}\\n696: \\n697: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n698: \\tif err != nil {\\n699: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\\n700: \\t}\\n701: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n702: \\n703: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeGetCapabilitiesRequest{}\\n704: \\tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeGetCapabilities(ctx, req)\\n705: \\tif err != nil {\\n706: \\t\\tif !isFinalError(err) {\\n707: \\t\\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\\n708: \\t\\t}\\n709: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\\n710: \\t}\\n711: \\treturn resp.GetCapabilities(), nil\\n712: }\\n713: \\n714: func isFinalError(err error) bool {\\n715: \\t// Sources:\\n716: \\t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md\\n717: \\t// https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/blob/master/spec.md\\n718: \\tst, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n719: \\tif !ok {\\n720: \\t\\t// This is not gRPC error. The operation must have failed before gRPC\\n721: \\t\\t// method was called, otherwise we would get gRPC error.\\n722: \\t\\t// We don't know if any previous volume operation is in progress, be on the safe side.\\n723: \\t\\treturn false\\n724: \\t}\\n725: \\tswitch st.Code() {\\n726: \\tcase codes.Canceled, // gRPC: Client Application cancelled the request\\n727: \\t\\tcodes.DeadlineExceeded,  // gRPC: Timeout\\n728: \\t\\tcodes.Unavailable,       // gRPC: Server shutting down, TCP connection broken - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\\n729: \\t\\tcodes.ResourceExhausted, // gRPC: Server temporarily out of resources - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\\n730: \\t\\tcodes.Aborted:           // CSI: Operation pending for volume\\n731: \\t\\treturn false\\n732: \\t}\\n733: \\t// All other errors mean that operation either did not\\n734: \\t// even start or failed. It is for sure not in progress.\\n735: \\treturn true\\n736: }\\n737: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--8c9c67c0@8c9c67c000104450cfc5a5f48053a9a84b73cf93/-/blob/pkg/volume/csi/csi_client.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package csi\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"io\"\n24: \t\"net\"\n25: \t\"strconv\"\n26: \t\"sync\"\n27: \n28: \tcsipbv1 \"github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/lib/go/csi\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n30: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n31: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\"\n32: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n33: \tapi \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n34: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\"\n35: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n37: \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n38: \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume\"\n39: \tvolumetypes \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/volume/util/types\"\n40: )\n41: \n42: type csiClient interface {\n43: \tNodeGetInfo(ctx context.Context) (\n44: \t\tnodeID string,\n45: \t\tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\n46: \t\taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\n47: \t\terr error)\n48: \n49: \t// The caller is responsible for checking whether the driver supports\n50: \t// applying FSGroup by calling NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup().\n51: \t// If the driver does not, fsGroup must be set to nil.\n52: \tNodePublishVolume(\n53: \t\tctx context.Context,\n54: \t\tvolumeid string,\n55: \t\treadOnly bool,\n56: \t\tstagingTargetPath string,\n57: \t\ttargetPath string,\n58: \t\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\n59: \t\tpublishContext map[string]string,\n60: \t\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\n61: \t\tsecrets map[string]string,\n62: \t\tfsType string,\n63: \t\tmountOptions []string,\n64: \t\tfsGroup *int64,\n65: \t) error\n66: \n67: \tNodeExpandVolume(ctx context.Context, rsOpts csiResizeOptions) (resource.Quantity, error)\n68: \tNodeUnpublishVolume(\n69: \t\tctx context.Context,\n70: \t\tvolID string,\n71: \t\ttargetPath string,\n72: \t) error\n73: \n74: \t// The caller is responsible for checking whether the driver supports\n75: \t// applying FSGroup by calling NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup().\n76: \t// If the driver does not, fsGroup must be set to nil.\n77: \tNodeStageVolume(ctx context.Context,\n78: \t\tvolID string,\n79: \t\tpublishVolumeInfo map[string]string,\n80: \t\tstagingTargetPath string,\n81: \t\tfsType string,\n82: \t\taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\n83: \t\tsecrets map[string]string,\n84: \t\tvolumeContext map[string]string,\n85: \t\tmountOptions []string,\n86: \t\tfsGroup *int64,\n87: \t) error\n88: \n89: \tNodeGetVolumeStats(\n90: \t\tctx context.Context,\n91: \t\tvolID string,\n92: \t\ttargetPath string,\n93: \t) (*volume.Metrics, error)\n94: \tNodeUnstageVolume(ctx context.Context, volID, stagingTargetPath string) error\n95: \tNodeSupportsStageUnstage(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\n96: \tNodeSupportsNodeExpand(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\n97: \tNodeSupportsVolumeStats(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\n98: \tNodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\n99: \tNodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)\n100: }\n101: \n102: // Strongly typed address\n103: type csiAddr string\n104: \n105: // Strongly typed driver name\n106: type csiDriverName string\n107: \n108: // csiClient encapsulates all csi-plugin methods\n109: type csiDriverClient struct {\n110: \tdriverName          csiDriverName\n111: \taddr                csiAddr\n112: \tmetricsManager      *MetricsManager\n113: \tnodeV1ClientCreator nodeV1ClientCreator\n114: }\n115: \n116: type csiResizeOptions struct {\n117: \tvolumeID          string\n118: \tvolumePath        string\n119: \tstagingTargetPath string\n120: \tfsType            string\n121: \taccessMode        api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode\n122: \tnewSize           resource.Quantity\n123: \tmountOptions      []string\n124: \tsecrets           map[string]string\n125: }\n126: \n127: var _ csiClient = &csiDriverClient{}\n128: \n129: type nodeV1ClientCreator func(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (\n130: \tnodeClient csipbv1.NodeClient,\n131: \tcloser io.Closer,\n132: \terr error,\n133: )\n134: \n135: type nodeV1AccessModeMapper func(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode\n136: \n137: // newV1NodeClient creates a new NodeClient with the internally used gRPC\n138: // connection set up. It also returns a closer which must be called to close\n139: // the gRPC connection when the NodeClient is not used anymore.\n140: // This is the default implementation for the nodeV1ClientCreator, used in\n141: // newCsiDriverClient.\n142: func newV1NodeClient(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (nodeClient csipbv1.NodeClient, closer io.Closer, err error) {\n143: \tvar conn *grpc.ClientConn\n144: \tconn, err = newGrpcConn(addr, metricsManager)\n145: \tif err != nil {\n146: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n147: \t}\n148: \n149: \tnodeClient = csipbv1.NewNodeClient(conn)\n150: \treturn nodeClient, conn, nil\n151: }\n152: \n153: func newCsiDriverClient(driverName csiDriverName) (*csiDriverClient, error) {\n154: \tif driverName == \"\" {\n155: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"driver name is empty\")\n156: \t}\n157: \n158: \texistingDriver, driverExists := csiDrivers.Get(string(driverName))\n159: \tif !driverExists {\n160: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"driver name %s not found in the list of registered CSI drivers\", driverName)\n161: \t}\n162: \n163: \tnodeV1ClientCreator := newV1NodeClient\n164: \treturn &csiDriverClient{\n165: \t\tdriverName:          driverName,\n166: \t\taddr:                csiAddr(existingDriver.endpoint),\n167: \t\tnodeV1ClientCreator: nodeV1ClientCreator,\n168: \t\tmetricsManager:      NewCSIMetricsManager(string(driverName)),\n169: \t}, nil\n170: }\n171: \n172: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeGetInfo(ctx context.Context) (\n173: \tnodeID string,\n174: \tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\n175: \taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\n176: \terr error) {\n177: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodeGetInfo rpc\"))\n178: \n179: \tvar getNodeInfoError error\n180: \tnodeID, maxVolumePerNode, accessibleTopology, getNodeInfoError = c.nodeGetInfoV1(ctx)\n181: \tif getNodeInfoError != nil {\n182: \t\tklog.InfoS(\"Error calling CSI NodeGetInfo()\", \"err\", getNodeInfoError.Error())\n183: \t}\n184: \treturn nodeID, maxVolumePerNode, accessibleTopology, getNodeInfoError\n185: }\n186: \n187: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeGetInfoV1(ctx context.Context) (\n188: \tnodeID string,\n189: \tmaxVolumePerNode int64,\n190: \taccessibleTopology map[string]string,\n191: \terr error) {\n192: \n193: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n194: \tif err != nil {\n195: \t\treturn \"\", 0, nil, err\n196: \t}\n197: \tdefer closer.Close()\n198: \n199: \tres, err := nodeClient.NodeGetInfo(ctx, &csipbv1.NodeGetInfoRequest{})\n200: \tif err != nil {\n201: \t\treturn \"\", 0, nil, err\n202: \t}\n203: \n204: \ttopology := res.GetAccessibleTopology()\n205: \tif topology != nil {\n206: \t\taccessibleTopology = topology.Segments\n207: \t}\n208: \treturn res.GetNodeId(), res.GetMaxVolumesPerNode(), accessibleTopology, nil\n209: }\n210: \n211: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodePublishVolume(\n212: \tctx context.Context,\n213: \tvolID string,\n214: \treadOnly bool,\n215: \tstagingTargetPath string,\n216: \ttargetPath string,\n217: \taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\n218: \tpublishContext map[string]string,\n219: \tvolumeContext map[string]string,\n220: \tsecrets map[string]string,\n221: \tfsType string,\n222: \tmountOptions []string,\n223: \tfsGroup *int64,\n224: ) error {\n225: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodePublishVolume rpc\"), \"volID\", volID, \"targetPath\", targetPath)\n226: \tif volID == \"\" {\n227: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n228: \t}\n229: \tif targetPath == \"\" {\n230: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing target path\")\n231: \t}\n232: \n233: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n234: \t\treturn errors.New(\"failed to call NodePublishVolume. nodeV1ClientCreator is nil\")\n235: \t}\n236: \n237: \taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\n238: \tif err != nil {\n239: \t\treturn err\n240: \t}\n241: \n242: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n243: \tif err != nil {\n244: \t\treturn err\n245: \t}\n246: \tdefer closer.Close()\n247: \n248: \treq := &csipbv1.NodePublishVolumeRequest{\n249: \t\tVolumeId:       volID,\n250: \t\tTargetPath:     targetPath,\n251: \t\tReadonly:       readOnly,\n252: \t\tPublishContext: publishContext,\n253: \t\tVolumeContext:  volumeContext,\n254: \t\tSecrets:        secrets,\n255: \t\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\n256: \t\t\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\n257: \t\t\t\tMode: accessModeMapper(accessMode),\n258: \t\t\t},\n259: \t\t},\n260: \t}\n261: \tif stagingTargetPath != \"\" {\n262: \t\treq.StagingTargetPath = stagingTargetPath\n263: \t}\n264: \n265: \tif fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\n266: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\n267: \t\t\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\n268: \t\t}\n269: \t} else {\n270: \t\tmountVolume := &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\n271: \t\t\tFsType:     fsType,\n272: \t\t\tMountFlags: mountOptions,\n273: \t\t}\n274: \t\tif fsGroup != nil {\n275: \t\t\tmountVolume.VolumeMountGroup = strconv.FormatInt(*fsGroup, 10 /* base */)\n276: \t\t}\n277: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\n278: \t\t\tMount: mountVolume,\n279: \t\t}\n280: \t}\n281: \n282: \t_, err = nodeClient.NodePublishVolume(ctx, req)\n283: \tif err != nil && !isFinalError(err) {\n284: \t\treturn volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\n285: \t}\n286: \treturn err\n287: }\n288: \n289: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeExpandVolume(ctx context.Context, opts csiResizeOptions) (resource.Quantity, error) {\n290: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n291: \t\treturn opts.newSize, fmt.Errorf(\"version of CSI driver does not support volume expansion\")\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tif opts.volumeID == \"\" {\n295: \t\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n296: \t}\n297: \tif opts.volumePath == \"\" {\n298: \t\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\"missing volume path\")\n299: \t}\n300: \n301: \tif opts.newSize.Value() < 0 {\n302: \t\treturn opts.newSize, errors.New(\"size can not be less than 0\")\n303: \t}\n304: \n305: \taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\n306: \tif err != nil {\n307: \t\treturn opts.newSize, err\n308: \t}\n309: \n310: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n311: \tif err != nil {\n312: \t\treturn opts.newSize, err\n313: \t}\n314: \tdefer closer.Close()\n315: \n316: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeExpandVolumeRequest{\n317: \t\tVolumeId:      opts.volumeID,\n318: \t\tVolumePath:    opts.volumePath,\n319: \t\tCapacityRange: &csipbv1.CapacityRange{RequiredBytes: opts.newSize.Value()},\n320: \t\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\n321: \t\t\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\n322: \t\t\t\tMode: accessModeMapper(opts.accessMode),\n323: \t\t\t},\n324: \t\t},\n325: \t\tSecrets: opts.secrets,\n326: \t}\n327: \n328: \t// not all CSI drivers support NodeStageUnstage and hence the StagingTargetPath\n329: \t// should only be set when available\n330: \tif opts.stagingTargetPath != \"\" {\n331: \t\treq.StagingTargetPath = opts.stagingTargetPath\n332: \t}\n333: \n334: \tif opts.fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\n335: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\n336: \t\t\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\n337: \t\t}\n338: \t} else {\n339: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\n340: \t\t\tMount: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\n341: \t\t\t\tFsType:     opts.fsType,\n342: \t\t\t\tMountFlags: opts.mountOptions,\n343: \t\t\t},\n344: \t\t}\n345: \t}\n346: \n347: \tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeExpandVolume(ctx, req)\n348: \tif err != nil {\n349: \t\tif !isFinalError(err) {\n350: \t\t\treturn opts.newSize, volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\n351: \t\t}\n352: \t\treturn opts.newSize, err\n353: \t}\n354: \n355: \tupdatedQuantity := resource.NewQuantity(resp.CapacityBytes, resource.BinarySI)\n356: \treturn *updatedQuantity, nil\n357: }\n358: \n359: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeUnpublishVolume(ctx context.Context, volID string, targetPath string) error {\n360: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodeUnpublishVolume rpc\"), \"volID\", volID, \"targetPath\", targetPath)\n361: \tif volID == \"\" {\n362: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n363: \t}\n364: \tif targetPath == \"\" {\n365: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing target path\")\n366: \t}\n367: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n368: \t\treturn errors.New(\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\")\n369: \t}\n370: \n371: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n372: \tif err != nil {\n373: \t\treturn err\n374: \t}\n375: \tdefer closer.Close()\n376: \n377: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeUnpublishVolumeRequest{\n378: \t\tVolumeId:   volID,\n379: \t\tTargetPath: targetPath,\n380: \t}\n381: \n382: \t_, err = nodeClient.NodeUnpublishVolume(ctx, req)\n383: \treturn err\n384: }\n385: \n386: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeStageVolume(ctx context.Context,\n387: \tvolID string,\n388: \tpublishContext map[string]string,\n389: \tstagingTargetPath string,\n390: \tfsType string,\n391: \taccessMode api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode,\n392: \tsecrets map[string]string,\n393: \tvolumeContext map[string]string,\n394: \tmountOptions []string,\n395: \tfsGroup *int64,\n396: ) error {\n397: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodeStageVolume rpc\"), \"volID\", volID, \"stagingTargetPath\", stagingTargetPath)\n398: \tif volID == \"\" {\n399: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n400: \t}\n401: \tif stagingTargetPath == \"\" {\n402: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing staging target path\")\n403: \t}\n404: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n405: \t\treturn errors.New(\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\")\n406: \t}\n407: \n408: \taccessModeMapper, err := c.getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx)\n409: \tif err != nil {\n410: \t\treturn err\n411: \t}\n412: \n413: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n414: \tif err != nil {\n415: \t\treturn err\n416: \t}\n417: \tdefer closer.Close()\n418: \n419: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeStageVolumeRequest{\n420: \t\tVolumeId:          volID,\n421: \t\tPublishContext:    publishContext,\n422: \t\tStagingTargetPath: stagingTargetPath,\n423: \t\tVolumeCapability: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability{\n424: \t\t\tAccessMode: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode{\n425: \t\t\t\tMode: accessModeMapper(accessMode),\n426: \t\t\t},\n427: \t\t},\n428: \t\tSecrets:       secrets,\n429: \t\tVolumeContext: volumeContext,\n430: \t}\n431: \n432: \tif fsType == fsTypeBlockName {\n433: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Block{\n434: \t\t\tBlock: &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_BlockVolume{},\n435: \t\t}\n436: \t} else {\n437: \t\tmountVolume := &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_MountVolume{\n438: \t\t\tFsType:     fsType,\n439: \t\t\tMountFlags: mountOptions,\n440: \t\t}\n441: \t\tif fsGroup != nil {\n442: \t\t\tmountVolume.VolumeMountGroup = strconv.FormatInt(*fsGroup, 10 /* base */)\n443: \t\t}\n444: \t\treq.VolumeCapability.AccessType = &csipbv1.VolumeCapability_Mount{\n445: \t\t\tMount: mountVolume,\n446: \t\t}\n447: \t}\n448: \n449: \t_, err = nodeClient.NodeStageVolume(ctx, req)\n450: \tif err != nil && !isFinalError(err) {\n451: \t\treturn volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\n452: \t}\n453: \treturn err\n454: }\n455: \n456: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeUnstageVolume(ctx context.Context, volID, stagingTargetPath string) error {\n457: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodeUnstageVolume rpc\"), \"volID\", volID, \"stagingTargetPath\", stagingTargetPath)\n458: \tif volID == \"\" {\n459: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n460: \t}\n461: \tif stagingTargetPath == \"\" {\n462: \t\treturn errors.New(\"missing staging target path\")\n463: \t}\n464: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n465: \t\treturn errors.New(\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\")\n466: \t}\n467: \n468: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n469: \tif err != nil {\n470: \t\treturn err\n471: \t}\n472: \tdefer closer.Close()\n473: \n474: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeUnstageVolumeRequest{\n475: \t\tVolumeId:          volID,\n476: \t\tStagingTargetPath: stagingTargetPath,\n477: \t}\n478: \t_, err = nodeClient.NodeUnstageVolume(ctx, req)\n479: \treturn err\n480: }\n481: \n482: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsNodeExpand(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n483: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_EXPAND_VOLUME)\n484: }\n485: \n486: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsStageUnstage(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n487: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_STAGE_UNSTAGE_VOLUME)\n488: }\n489: \n490: func (c *csiDriverClient) getNodeV1AccessModeMapper(ctx context.Context) (nodeV1AccessModeMapper, error) {\n491: \tsupported, err := c.NodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx)\n492: \tif err != nil {\n493: \t\treturn nil, err\n494: \t}\n495: \tif supported {\n496: \t\treturn asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1, nil\n497: \t}\n498: \treturn asCSIAccessModeV1, nil\n499: }\n500: \n501: func asCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\n502: \tswitch am {\n503: \tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\n504: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\n505: \tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\n506: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\n507: \tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\n508: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n509: \t// This mapping exists to enable CSI drivers that lack the\n510: \t// SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER capability to work with the\n511: \t// ReadWriteOncePod access mode.\n512: \tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\n513: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\n514: \t}\n515: \treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\n516: }\n517: \n518: func asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\n519: \tswitch am {\n520: \tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\n521: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n522: \tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\n523: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\n524: \tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\n525: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n526: \tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\n527: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_SINGLE_WRITER\n528: \t}\n529: \treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\n530: }\n531: \n532: func newGrpcConn(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n533: \tnetwork := \"unix\"\n534: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"creating new gRPC connection\"), \"protocol\", network, \"endpoint\", addr)\n535: \n536: \treturn grpc.Dial(\n537: \t\tstring(addr),\n538: \t\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\"localhost\"),\n539: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n540: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, target string) (net.Conn, error) {\n541: \t\t\treturn (&net.Dialer{}).DialContext(ctx, network, target)\n542: \t\t}),\n543: \t\tgrpc.WithChainUnaryInterceptor(metricsManager.RecordMetricsInterceptor),\n544: \t)\n545: }\n546: \n547: // CSI client getter with cache.\n548: // This provides a method to initialize CSI client with driver name and caches\n549: // it for later use. When CSI clients have not been discovered yet (e.g.\n550: // on kubelet restart), client initialization will fail. Users of CSI client (e.g.\n551: // mounter manager and block mapper) can use this to delay CSI client\n552: // initialization until needed.\n553: type csiClientGetter struct {\n554: \tsync.RWMutex\n555: \tcsiClient  csiClient\n556: \tdriverName csiDriverName\n557: }\n558: \n559: func (c *csiClientGetter) Get() (csiClient, error) {\n560: \tc.RLock()\n561: \tif c.csiClient != nil {\n562: \t\tc.RUnlock()\n563: \t\treturn c.csiClient, nil\n564: \t}\n565: \tc.RUnlock()\n566: \tc.Lock()\n567: \tdefer c.Unlock()\n568: \t// Double-checking locking criterion.\n569: \tif c.csiClient != nil {\n570: \t\treturn c.csiClient, nil\n571: \t}\n572: \tcsi, err := newCsiDriverClient(c.driverName)\n573: \tif err != nil {\n574: \t\treturn nil, err\n575: \t}\n576: \tc.csiClient = csi\n577: \treturn c.csiClient, nil\n578: }\n579: \n580: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsVolumeStats(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n581: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_GET_VOLUME_STATS)\n582: }\n583: \n584: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n585: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER)\n586: }\n587: \n588: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeGetVolumeStats(ctx context.Context, volID string, targetPath string) (*volume.Metrics, error) {\n589: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"calling NodeGetVolumeStats rpc\"), \"volID\", volID, \"targetPath\", targetPath)\n590: \tif volID == \"\" {\n591: \t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"missing volume id\")\n592: \t}\n593: \tif targetPath == \"\" {\n594: \t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"missing target path\")\n595: \t}\n596: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n597: \t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\")\n598: \t}\n599: \n600: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n601: \tif err != nil {\n602: \t\treturn nil, err\n603: \t}\n604: \tdefer closer.Close()\n605: \n606: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeGetVolumeStatsRequest{\n607: \t\tVolumeId:   volID,\n608: \t\tVolumePath: targetPath,\n609: \t}\n610: \n611: \tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeGetVolumeStats(ctx, req)\n612: \tif err != nil {\n613: \t\treturn nil, err\n614: \t}\n615: \n616: \tmetrics := &volume.Metrics{\n617: \t\tUsed:       resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n618: \t\tCapacity:   resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n619: \t\tAvailable:  resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n620: \t\tInodesUsed: resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n621: \t\tInodes:     resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n622: \t\tInodesFree: resource.NewQuantity(int64(0), resource.BinarySI),\n623: \t}\n624: \n625: \tvar isSupportNodeVolumeCondition bool\n626: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CSIVolumeHealth) {\n627: \t\tisSupportNodeVolumeCondition, err = c.nodeSupportsVolumeCondition(ctx)\n628: \t\tif err != nil {\n629: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n630: \t\t}\n631: \n632: \t\tif isSupportNodeVolumeCondition {\n633: \t\t\tabnormal, message := resp.VolumeCondition.GetAbnormal(), resp.VolumeCondition.GetMessage()\n634: \t\t\tmetrics.Abnormal, metrics.Message = &abnormal, &message\n635: \t\t}\n636: \t}\n637: \n638: \tusages := resp.GetUsage()\n639: \t// If the driver does not support volume condition and usages is nil, return an error\n640: \tif !isSupportNodeVolumeCondition && usages == nil {\n641: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get usage from response. usage is nil\")\n642: \t}\n643: \n644: \tfor _, usage := range usages {\n645: \t\tif usage == nil {\n646: \t\t\tcontinue\n647: \t\t}\n648: \t\tunit := usage.GetUnit()\n649: \t\tswitch unit {\n650: \t\tcase csipbv1.VolumeUsage_BYTES:\n651: \t\t\tmetrics.Available = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetAvailable(), resource.BinarySI)\n652: \t\t\tmetrics.Capacity = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetTotal(), resource.BinarySI)\n653: \t\t\tmetrics.Used = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetUsed(), resource.BinarySI)\n654: \t\tcase csipbv1.VolumeUsage_INODES:\n655: \t\t\tmetrics.InodesFree = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetAvailable(), resource.BinarySI)\n656: \t\t\tmetrics.Inodes = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetTotal(), resource.BinarySI)\n657: \t\t\tmetrics.InodesUsed = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetUsed(), resource.BinarySI)\n658: \t\tdefault:\n659: \t\t\tklog.ErrorS(nil, \"unknown unit in VolumeUsage\", \"unit\", unit.String())\n660: \t\t}\n661: \n662: \t}\n663: \treturn metrics, nil\n664: }\n665: \n666: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeSupportsVolumeCondition(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n667: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_VOLUME_CONDITION)\n668: }\n669: \n670: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n671: \treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_VOLUME_MOUNT_GROUP)\n672: }\n673: \n674: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeSupportsCapability(ctx context.Context, capabilityType csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_Type) (bool, error) {\n675: \tklog.V(4).Info(log(\"calling NodeGetCapabilities rpc to determine if the node service has %s capability\", capabilityType))\n676: \tcapabilities, err := c.nodeGetCapabilities(ctx)\n677: \tif err != nil {\n678: \t\treturn false, err\n679: \t}\n680: \n681: \tfor _, capability := range capabilities {\n682: \t\tif capability == nil || capability.GetRpc() == nil {\n683: \t\t\tcontinue\n684: \t\t}\n685: \t\tif capability.GetRpc().GetType() == capabilityType {\n686: \t\t\treturn true, nil\n687: \t\t}\n688: \t}\n689: \treturn false, nil\n690: }\n691: \n692: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeGetCapabilities(ctx context.Context) ([]*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability, error) {\n693: \tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\n694: \t\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, errors.New(\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\")\n695: \t}\n696: \n697: \tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\n698: \tif err != nil {\n699: \t\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\n700: \t}\n701: \tdefer closer.Close()\n702: \n703: \treq := &csipbv1.NodeGetCapabilitiesRequest{}\n704: \tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeGetCapabilities(ctx, req)\n705: \tif err != nil {\n706: \t\tif !isFinalError(err) {\n707: \t\t\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\n708: \t\t}\n709: \t\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\n710: \t}\n711: \treturn resp.GetCapabilities(), nil\n712: }\n713: \n714: func isFinalError(err error) bool {\n715: \t// Sources:\n716: \t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md\n717: \t// https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/blob/master/spec.md\n718: \tst, ok := status.FromError(err)\n719: \tif !ok {\n720: \t\t// This is not gRPC error. The operation must have failed before gRPC\n721: \t\t// method was called, otherwise we would get gRPC error.\n722: \t\t// We don't know if any previous volume operation is in progress, be on the safe side.\n723: \t\treturn false\n724: \t}\n725: \tswitch st.Code() {\n726: \tcase codes.Canceled, // gRPC: Client Application cancelled the request\n727: \t\tcodes.DeadlineExceeded,  // gRPC: Timeout\n728: \t\tcodes.Unavailable,       // gRPC: Server shutting down, TCP connection broken - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\n729: \t\tcodes.ResourceExhausted, // gRPC: Server temporarily out of resources - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\n730: \t\tcodes.Aborted:           // CSI: Operation pending for volume\n731: \t\treturn false\n732: \t}\n733: \t// All other errors mean that operation either did not\n734: \t// even start or failed. It is for sure not in progress.\n735: \treturn true\n736: }\n737: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--8c9c67c0@8c9c67c000104450cfc5a5f48053a9a84b73cf93/-/blob/pkg/volume/csi/csi_client.go"
  }
}

func NewRemoteImageService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.ImageManagerService, error) {\\n52: \\tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \\\"Connecting to image service\\\", \\\"endpoint\\\", endpoint)\\n53: \\taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\\n54: \\tif err != nil {\\n55: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\\n59: \\tdefer cancel()\\n60: \\n61: \\tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\\n62: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n63: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n64: \\t\\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\\\"localhost\\\"),\\n65: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\\n66: \\t\\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\\n67: \\tif tp != nil {\\n68: \\t\\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\\n69: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\\n70: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\\n71: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\\n72: \\t\\t}\\n73: \\t\\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\\n74: \\t\\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\\n75: \\t\\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n76: \\t\\t\\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\\n77: \\t}\\n78: \\n79: \\tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\\n80: \\t\\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\\n81: \\t}\\n82: \\tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\\n83: \\tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\\n84: \\tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\\n85: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n86: \\t\\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\\n87: \\t)\\n88: \\n89: \\tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\\n90: \\tif err != nil {\\n91: \\t\\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \\\"Connect remote image service failed\\\", \\\"address\\\", addr)\\n92: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n93: \\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\tservice := &remoteImageService{\\n96: \\t\\ttimeout: connectionTimeout,\\n97: \\t\\tlogger:  logger,\\n98: \\t\\tconn:    conn,\\n99: \\t}\\n100: \\tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\\n101: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate service connection: %w\\\", err)\\n102: \\t}\\n103: \\n104: \\treturn service, nil\\n105: \\n106: }\\n107: \\n108: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\\n109: func (r *remoteImageService) Close() error {\\n110: \\tr.log(3, \\\"Closing image service connection\\\")\\n111: \\treturn r.conn.Close()\\n112: }\\n113: \\n114: func (r *remoteImageService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n115: \\tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: func (r *remoteImageService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n119: \\tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote image service by\\n123: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\\n124: func (r *remoteImageService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\\n125: \\tr.log(4, \\\"Validating the CRI v1 API image version\\\")\\n126: \\tr.imageClient = runtimeapi.NewImageServiceClient(conn)\\n127: \\n128: \\tif _, err := r.imageClient.ImageFsInfo(ctx, &runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoRequest{}); err != nil {\\n129: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate CRI v1 image API for endpoint %q: %w\\\", endpoint, err)\\n130: \\t}\\n131: \\n132: \\tr.log(2, \\\"Validated CRI v1 image API\\\")\\n133: \\treturn nil\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // ListImages lists available images.\\n137: func (r *remoteImageService) ListImages(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ImageFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Image, error) {\\n138: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n139: \\tdefer cancel()\\n140: \\n141: \\treturn r.listImagesV1(ctx, filter)\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: func (r *remoteImageService) listImagesV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ImageFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Image, error) {\\n145: \\tresp, err := r.imageClient.ListImages(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListImagesRequest{\\n146: \\t\\tFilter: filter,\\n147: \\t})\\n148: \\tif err != nil {\\n149: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListImages with filter from image service failed\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n150: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\treturn resp.Images, nil\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: // ImageStatus returns the status of the image.\\n157: func (r *remoteImageService) ImageStatus(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ImageStatusResponse, error) {\\n158: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n159: \\tdefer cancel()\\n160: \\n161: \\treturn r.imageStatusV1(ctx, image, verbose)\\n162: }\\n163: \\n164: func (r *remoteImageService) imageStatusV1(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ImageStatusResponse, error) {\\n165: \\tresp, err := r.imageClient.ImageStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.ImageStatusRequest{\\n166: \\t\\tImage:   image,\\n167: \\t\\tVerbose: verbose,\\n168: \\t})\\n169: \\tif err != nil {\\n170: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Get ImageStatus from image service failed\\\", \\\"image\\\", image.Image)\\n171: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\n174: \\tif resp.Image != nil {\\n175: \\t\\tif resp.Image.Id == \\\"\\\" || resp.Image.Size == 0 {\\n176: \\t\\t\\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Id or size of image %q is not set\\\", image.Image)\\n177: \\t\\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n178: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ImageStatus failed\\\", \\\"image\\\", image.Image)\\n179: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n180: \\t\\t}\\n181: \\t}\\n182: \\n183: \\treturn resp, nil\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: // PullImage pulls an image with authentication config.\\n187: func (r *remoteImageService) PullImage(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, auth *runtimeapi.AuthConfig, podSandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\\n188: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\\n189: \\tdefer cancel()\\n190: \\n191: \\treturn r.pullImageV1(ctx, image, auth, podSandboxConfig)\\n192: }\\n193: \\n194: func (r *remoteImageService) pullImageV1(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, auth *runtimeapi.AuthConfig, podSandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\\n195: \\tresp, err := r.imageClient.PullImage(ctx, &runtimeapi.PullImageRequest{\\n196: \\t\\tImage:         image,\\n197: \\t\\tAuth:          auth,\\n198: \\t\\tSandboxConfig: podSandboxConfig,\\n199: \\t})\\n200: \\tif err != nil {\\n201: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"PullImage from image service failed\\\", \\\"image\\\", image.Image)\\n202: \\n203: \\t\\t// We can strip the code from unknown status errors since they add no value\\n204: \\t\\t// and will make them easier to read in the logs/events.\\n205: \\t\\t//\\n206: \\t\\t// It also ensures that checking custom error types from pkg/kubelet/images/types.go\\n207: \\t\\t// works in `imageManager.EnsureImageExists` (pkg/kubelet/images/image_manager.go).\\n208: \\t\\tstatusErr, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n209: \\t\\tif ok && statusErr.Code() == codes.Unknown {\\n210: \\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", errors.New(statusErr.Message())\\n211: \\t\\t}\\n212: \\n213: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n214: \\t}\\n215: \\n216: \\tif resp.ImageRef == \\\"\\\" {\\n217: \\t\\tr.logErr(errors.New(\\\"PullImage failed\\\"), \\\"ImageRef of image is not set\\\", \\\"image\\\", image.Image)\\n218: \\t\\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"imageRef of image %q is not set\\\", image.Image)\\n219: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", errors.New(errorMessage)\\n220: \\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\treturn resp.ImageRef, nil\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: // RemoveImage removes the image.\\n226: func (r *remoteImageService) RemoveImage(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec) (err error) {\\n227: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n228: \\tdefer cancel()\\n229: \\n230: failed\\\", \\\"image\\\", image.Image)\\n234: \\t\\treturn err\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\treturn nil\\n238: }\\n239: \\n240: // ImageFsInfo returns information of the filesystem that is used to store images.\\n241: func (r *remoteImageService) ImageFsInfo(ctx context.Context) (*runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoResponse, error) {\\n242: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n243: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn resp, nil\\n255: }\\n256: NewRemoteImageService creates a new internalapi.ImageManagerService.\n51: func NewRemoteImageService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.ImageManagerService, error) {\n52: \tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \"Connecting to image service\", \"endpoint\", endpoint)\n53: \taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\n54: \tif err != nil {\n55: \t\treturn nil, err\n56: \t}\n57: \n58: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\n59: \tdefer cancel()\n60: \n61: \tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\n62: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n63: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n64: \t\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\"localhost\"),\n65: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\n66: \t\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\n67: \tif tp != nil {\n68: \t\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\n69: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\n70: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\n71: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\n72: \t\t}\n73: \t\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\n74: \t\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\n75: \t\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n76: \t\t\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\n77: \t}\n78: \n79: \tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\n80: \t\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\n81: \t}\n82: \tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\n83: \tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\n84: \tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\n85: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n86: \t\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\n87: \t)\n88: \n89: \tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\n90: \tif err != nil {\n91: \t\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \"Connect remote image service failed\", \"address\", addr)\n92: \t\treturn nil, err\n93: \t}\n94: \n95: \tservice := &remoteImageService{\n96: \t\ttimeout: connectionTimeout,\n97: \t\tlogger:  logger,\n98: \t\tconn:    conn,\n99: \t}\n100: \tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\n101: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"validate service connection: %w\", err)\n102: \t}\n103: \n104: \treturn service, nil\n105: \n106: }\n107: \n108: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\n109: func (r *remoteImageService) Close() error {\n110: \tr.log(3, \"Closing image service connection\")\n111: \treturn r.conn.Close()\n112: }\n113: \n114: func (r *remoteImageService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n115: \tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\n116: }\n117: \n118: func (r *remoteImageService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n119: \tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\n120: }\n121: \n122: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote image service by\n123: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\n124: func (r *remoteImageService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\n125: \tr.log(4, \"Validating the CRI v1 API image version\")\n126: \tr.imageClient = runtimeapi.NewImageServiceClient(conn)\n127: \n128: \tif _, err := r.imageClient.ImageFsInfo(ctx, &runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoRequest{}); err != nil {\n129: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"validate CRI v1 image API for endpoint %q: %w\", endpoint, err)\n130: \t}\n131: \n132: \tr.log(2, \"Validated CRI v1 image API\")\n133: \treturn nil\n134: }\n135: \n136: // ListImages lists available images.\n137: func (r *remoteImageService) ListImages(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ImageFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Image, error) {\n138: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n139: \tdefer cancel()\n140: \n141: \treturn r.listImagesV1(ctx, filter)\n142: }\n143: \n144: func (r *remoteImageService) listImagesV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ImageFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Image, error) {\n145: \tresp, err := r.imageClient.ListImages(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListImagesRequest{\n146: \t\tFilter: filter,\n147: \t})\n148: \tif err != nil {\n149: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ListImages with filter from image service failed\", \"filter\", filter)\n150: \t\treturn nil, err\n151: \t}\n152: \n153: \treturn resp.Images, nil\n154: }\n155: \n156: // ImageStatus returns the status of the image.\n157: func (r *remoteImageService) ImageStatus(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ImageStatusResponse, error) {\n158: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n159: \tdefer cancel()\n160: \n161: \treturn r.imageStatusV1(ctx, image, verbose)\n162: }\n163: \n164: func (r *remoteImageService) imageStatusV1(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ImageStatusResponse, error) {\n165: \tresp, err := r.imageClient.ImageStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.ImageStatusRequest{\n166: \t\tImage:   image,\n167: \t\tVerbose: verbose,\n168: \t})\n169: \tif err != nil {\n170: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Get ImageStatus from image service failed\", \"image\", image.Image)\n171: \t\treturn nil, err\n172: \t}\n173: \n174: \tif resp.Image != nil {\n175: \t\tif resp.Image.Id == \"\" || resp.Image.Size == 0 {\n176: \t\t\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\"Id or size of image %q is not set\", image.Image)\n177: \t\t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n178: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"ImageStatus failed\", \"image\", image.Image)\n179: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n180: \t\t}\n181: \t}\n182: \n183: \treturn resp, nil\n184: }\n185: \n186: // PullImage pulls an image with authentication config.\n187: func (r *remoteImageService) PullImage(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, auth *runtimeapi.AuthConfig, podSandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\n188: \tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n189: \tdefer cancel()\n190: \n191: \treturn r.pullImageV1(ctx, image, auth, podSandboxConfig)\n192: }\n193: \n194: func (r *remoteImageService) pullImageV1(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec, auth *runtimeapi.AuthConfig, podSandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\n195: \tresp, err := r.imageClient.PullImage(ctx, &runtimeapi.PullImageRequest{\n196: \t\tImage:         image,\n197: \t\tAuth:          auth,\n198: \t\tSandboxConfig: podSandboxConfig,\n199: \t})\n200: \tif err != nil {\n201: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"PullImage from image service failed\", \"image\", image.Image)\n202: \n203: \t\t// We can strip the code from unknown status errors since they add no value\n204: \t\t// and will make them easier to read in the logs/events.\n205: \t\t//\n206: \t\t// It also ensures that checking custom error types from pkg/kubelet/images/types.go\n207: \t\t// works in `imageManager.EnsureImageExists` (pkg/kubelet/images/image_manager.go).\n208: \t\tstatusErr, ok := status.FromError(err)\n209: \t\tif ok && statusErr.Code() == codes.Unknown {\n210: \t\t\treturn \"\", errors.New(statusErr.Message())\n211: \t\t}\n212: \n213: \t\treturn \"\", err\n214: \t}\n215: \n216: \tif resp.ImageRef == \"\" {\n217: \t\tr.logErr(errors.New(\"PullImage failed\"), \"ImageRef of image is not set\", \"image\", image.Image)\n218: \t\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\"imageRef of image %q is not set\", image.Image)\n219: \t\treturn \"\", errors.New(errorMessage)\n220: \t}\n221: \n222: \treturn resp.ImageRef, nil\n223: }\n224: \n225: // RemoveImage removes the image.\n226: func (r *remoteImageService) RemoveImage(ctx context.Context, image *runtimeapi.ImageSpec) (err error) {\n227: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n228: \tdefer cancel()\n229: \n230: \tif _, err = r.imageClient.RemoveImage(ctx, &runtimeapi.RemoveImageRequest{\n231: \t\tImage: image,\n232: \t}); err != nil {\n233: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"RemoveImage from image service failed\", \"image\", image.Image)\n234: \t\treturn err\n235: \t}\n236: \n237: \treturn nil\n238: }\n239: \n240: // ImageFsInfo returns information of the filesystem that is used to store images.\n241: func (r *remoteImageService) ImageFsInfo(ctx context.Context) (*runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoResponse, error) {\n242: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n243: \tdefer cancel()\n244: \n245: \treturn r.imageFsInfoV1(ctx)\n246: }\n247: \n248: func (r *remoteImageService) imageFsInfoV1(ctx context.Context) (*runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoResponse, error) {\n249: \tresp, err := r.imageClient.ImageFsInfo(ctx, &runtimeapi.ImageFsInfoRequest{})\n250: \tif err != nil {\n251: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ImageFsInfo from image service failed\")\n252: \t\treturn nil, err\n253: \t}\n254: \treturn resp, nil\n255: }\n256: ",

the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package cri\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"io\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/google.golang.org/grpc/otelgrpc\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/backoff\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n34: \\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/logs/logreduction\\\"\\n36: \\ttracing \\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n37: \\tinternalapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis\\\"\\n38: \\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n40: \\tutilexec \\\"k8s.io/utils/exec\\\"\\n41: \\n42: \\t\\\"k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/internal\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/util\\\"\\n44: )\\n45: \\n46: // remoteRuntimeService is a gRPC implementation of internalapi.RuntimeService.\\n47: type remoteRuntimeService struct {\\n48: \\ttimeout       time.Duration\\n49: \\truntimeClient runtimeapi.RuntimeServiceClient\\n50: \\t// Cache last per-container error message to reduce log spam\\n51: \\tlogReduction *logreduction.LogReduction\\n52: \\tlogger       *klog.Logger\\n53: \\tconn         *grpc.ClientConn\\n54: }\\n55: \\n56: const (\\n57: \\t// How frequently to report identical errors\\n58: \\tidenticalErrorDelay = 1 * time.Minute\\n59: \\n60: \\t// connection parameters\\n61: \\tmaxBackoffDelay      = 3 * time.Second\\n62: \\tbaseBackoffDelay     = 100 * time.Millisecond\\n63: \\tminConnectionTimeout = 5 * time.Second\\n64: )\\n65: \\n66: // CRIVersion is the type for valid Container Runtime Interface (CRI) API\\n67: // versions.\\n68: type CRIVersion string\\n69: \\n70: var (\\n71: \\t// ErrContainerStatusNil indicates that the returned container status is nil.\\n72: \\tErrContainerStatusNil = errors.New(\\\"container status is nil\\\")\\n73: \\n74: \\t// ErrCommandTimedOut indicates that the exec sync command timed.\\n75: \\tErrCommandTimedOut = errors.New(\\\"command timed out\\\")\\n76: )\\n77: \\n78: const (\\n79: \\t// CRIVersionV1 references the v1 CRI API.\\n80: \\tCRIVersionV1 CRIVersion = \\\"v1\\\"\\n81: )\\n82: \\n83: // NewRemoteRuntimeService creates a new internalapi.RuntimeService.\\n84: func NewRemoteRuntimeService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.RuntimeService, error) {\\n85: \\tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \\\"Connecting to runtime service\\\", \\\"endpoint\\\", endpoint)\\n86: \\taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\\n87: \\tif err != nil {\\n88: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n89: \\t}\\n90: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\\n91: \\tdefer cancel()\\n92: \\n93: \\tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\\n94: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n95: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n96: \\t\\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\\\"localhost\\\"),\\n97: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\\n98: \\t\\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\\n99: \\tif tp != nil {\\n100: \\t\\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\\n101: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\\n102: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\\n103: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\\n106: \\t\\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\\n107: \\t\\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\\n112: \\t\\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\\n115: \\tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\\n116: \\tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\\n117: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n118: \\t\\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\\n119: \\t)\\n120: \\n121: \\tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\\n122: \\tif err != nil {\\n123: \\t\\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \\\"Connect remote runtime failed\\\", \\\"address\\\", addr)\\n124: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n125: \\t}\\n126: \\n127: \\tservice := &remoteRuntimeService{\\n128:
