\\t\\ttimeout:      connectionTimeout,\\n129: \\t\\tlogReduction: logreduction.NewLogReduction(identicalErrorDelay),\\n130: \\t\\tlogger:       logger,\\n131: \\t\\tconn:         conn,\\n132: \\t}\\n133: \\n134: \\tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\\n135: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate service connection: %w\\\", err)\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\treturn service, nil\\n139: }\\n140: \\n141: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\\n142: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Close() error {\\n143: \\tr.log(3, \\\"Closing runtime service connection\\\")\\n144: \\treturn r.conn.Close()\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n148: \\tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n149: }\\n150: \\n151: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n152: \\tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote runtime service by\\n156: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\\n157: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\\n158: \\tr.log(4, \\\"Validating the CRI v1 API runtime version\\\")\\n159: \\tr.runtimeClient = runtimeapi.NewRuntimeServiceClient(conn)\\n160: \\n161: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.Version(ctx, &runtimeapi.VersionRequest{}); err != nil {\\n162: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate CRI v1 runtime API for endpoint %q: %w\\\", endpoint, err)\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\tr.log(2, \\\"Validated CRI v1 runtime API\\\")\\n166: \\treturn nil\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: // Version returns the runtime name, runtime version and runtime API version.\\n170: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Version(ctx context.Context, apiVersion string) (*runtimeapi.VersionResponse, error) {\\n171: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Version\\\", \\\"apiVersion\\\", apiVersion, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n172: \\n173: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n174: \\tdefer cancel()\\n175: \\n176: \\treturn r.versionV1(ctx, apiVersion)\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) versionV1(ctx context.Context, apiVersion string) (*runtimeapi.VersionResponse, error) {\\n180: \\ttypedVersion, err := r.runtimeClient.Version(ctx, &runtimeapi.VersionRequest{\\n181: \\t\\tVersion: apiVersion,\\n182: \\t})\\n183: \\tif err != nil {\\n184: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Version from runtime service failed\\\")\\n185: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n186: \\t}\\n187: \\n188: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Version Response\\\", \\\"apiVersion\\\", typedVersion)\\n189: \\n190: \\tvar missingFields []string\\n191: \\tif typedVersion.Version == \\\"\\\" {\\n192: \\t\\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \\\"Version\\\")\\n193: \\t}\\n194: \\tif typedVersion.RuntimeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n195: \\t\\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \\\"RuntimeName\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\tif typedVersion.RuntimeApiVersion == \\\"\\\" {\\n198: \\t\\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \\\"RuntimeApiVersion\\\")\\n199: \\t}\\n200: \\tif typedVersion.RuntimeVersion == \\\"\\\" {\\n201: \\t\\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \\\"RuntimeVersion\\\")\\n202: \\t}\\n203: \\tif len(missingFields) > 0 {\\n204: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"not all fields are set in VersionResponse (missing %s)\\\", strings.Join(missingFields, \\\", \\\"))\\n205: \\t}\\n206: \\n207: \\treturn typedVersion, err\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: // RunPodSandbox creates and starts a pod-level sandbox. Runtimes should ensure\\n211: // the sandbox is in ready state.\\n212: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RunPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, config *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig, runtimeHandler string) (string, error) {\\n213: \\t// Use 2 times longer timeout for sandbox operation (4 mins by default)\\n214: \\t// TODO: Make the pod sandbox timeout configurable.\\n215: \\ttimeout := r.timeout * 2\\n216: \\n217: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RunPodSandbox\\\", \\\"config\\\", config, \\\"runtimeHandler\\\", runtimeHandler, \\\"timeout\\\", timeout)\\n218: \\n219: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\\n220: \\tdefer cancel()\\n221: \\n222: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.RunPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.RunPodSandboxRequest{\\n223: \\t\\tConfig:         config,\\n224: \\t\\tRuntimeHandler: runtimeHandler,\\n225: \\t})\\n226: \\n227: \\tif err != nil {\\n228: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"RunPodSandbox from runtime service failed\\\")\\n229: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n230: \\t}\\n231: \\n232: \\tpodSandboxID := resp.PodSandboxId\\n233: \\n234: \\tif podSandboxID == \\\"\\\" {\\n235: \\t\\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"PodSandboxId is not set for sandbox %q\\\", config.Metadata)\\n236: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n237: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"RunPodSandbox failed\\\")\\n238: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n239: \\t}\\n240: \\n241: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RunPodSandbox Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandboxID)\\n242: \\n243: \\treturn podSandboxID, nil\\n244: }\\n245: \\n246: // StopPodSandbox stops the sandbox. If there are any running containers in the\\n247: // sandbox, they should be forced to termination.\\n248: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StopPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string) (err error) {\\n249: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopPodSandbox\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n250: \\n251: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n252: \\tdefer cancel()\\n253: \\n254: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StopPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.StopPodSandboxRequest{\\n255: \\t\\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\\n256: \\t}); err != nil {\\n257: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"StopPodSandbox from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID)\\n258: \\t\\treturn err\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopPodSandbox Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID)\\n262: \\n263: \\treturn nil\\n264: }\\n265: \\n266: // RemovePodSandbox removes the sandbox. If there are any containers in the\\n267: // sandbox, they should be forcibly removed.\\n268: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RemovePodSandbox(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string) (err error) {\\n269: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemovePodSandbox\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n270: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n271: \\tdefer cancel()\\n272: \\n273: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.RemovePodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.RemovePodSandboxRequest{\\n274: \\t\\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\\n275: \\t}); err != nil {\\n276: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"RemovePodSandbox from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID)\\n277: \\t\\treturn err\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemovePodSandbox Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID)\\n281: \\n282: \\treturn nil\\n283: }\\n284: \\n285: // PodSandboxStatus returns the status of the PodSandbox.\\n286: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PodSandboxStatus(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusResponse, error) {\\n287: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStatus\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n288: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n289: \\tdefer cancel()\\n290: \\n291: \\treturn r.podSandboxStatusV1(ctx, podSandBoxID, verbose)\\n292: }\\n293: \\n294: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) podSandboxStatusV1(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusResponse, error) {\\n295: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PodSandboxStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusRequest{\\n296: \\t\\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\\n297: \\t\\tVerbose:      verbose,\\n298: \\t})\\n299: \\tif err != nil {\\n300: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n301: \\t}\\n302: \\n303: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStatus Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"status\\\", resp.Status)\\n304: \\n305: \\tstatus := resp.Status\\n306: \\tif resp.Status != nil {\\n307: \\t\\tif err := verifySandboxStatus(status); err != nil {\\n308: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n309: \\t\\t}\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\n312: \\treturn resp, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // ListPodSandbox returns a list of PodSandboxes.\\n316: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandbox, error) {\\n317: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandbox\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n318: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n319: \\tdefer cancel()\\n320: \\n321: \\treturn r.listPodSandboxV1(ctx, filter)\\n322: }\\n323: \\n324: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listPodSandboxV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandbox, error) {\\n325: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListPodSandboxRequest{\\n326: \\t\\tFilter: filter,\\n327: \\t})\\n328: \\tif err != nil {\\n329: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListPodSandbox with filter from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n330: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n331: \\t}\\n332: \\n333: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandbox Response\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"items\\\", resp.Items)\\n334: \\n335: \\treturn resp.Items, nil\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: // CreateContainer creates a new container in the specified PodSandbox.\\n339: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) CreateContainer(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, config *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig, sandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\\n340: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CreateContainer\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n341: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n342: \\tdefer cancel()\\n343: \\n344: \\treturn r.createContainerV1(ctx, podSandBoxID, config, sandboxConfig)\\n345: }\\n346: \\n347: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) createContainerV1(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, config *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig, sandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\\n348: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.CreateContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.CreateContainerRequest{\\n349: \\t\\tPodSandboxId:  podSandBoxID,\\n350: \\t\\tConfig:        config,\\n351: \\t\\tSandboxConfig: sandboxConfig,\\n352: \\t})\\n353: \\tif err != nil {\\n354: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"CreateContainer in sandbox from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID)\\n355: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n356: \\t}\\n357: \\n358: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CreateContainer\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandBoxID, \\\"containerID\\\", resp.ContainerId)\\n359: \\tif resp.ContainerId == \\\"\\\" {\\n360: \\t\\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"ContainerId is not set for container %q\\\", config.Metadata)\\n361: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n362: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"CreateContainer failed\\\")\\n363: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n364: \\t}\\n365: \\n366: \\treturn resp.ContainerId, nil\\n367: }\\n368: \\n369: // StartContainer starts the container.\\n370: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StartContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\\n371: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StartContainer\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n372: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n373: \\tdefer cancel()\\n374: \\n375: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StartContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.StartContainerRequest{\\n376: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n377: \\t}); err != nil {\\n378: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"StartContainer from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n379: \\t\\treturn err\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StartContainer Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n382: \\n383: \\treturn nil\\n384: }\\n385: \\n386: // StopContainer stops a running container with a grace period (i.e., timeout).\\n387: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StopContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string, timeout int64) (err error) {\\n388: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopContainer\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", timeout)\\n389: \\t// Use timeout + default timeout (2 minutes) as timeout to leave extra time\\n390: \\t// for SIGKILL container and request latency.\\n391: \\tt := r.timeout + time.Duration(timeout)*time.Second\\n392: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, t)\\n393: \\tdefer cancel()\\n394: \\n395: \\tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\\n396: \\n397: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StopContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.StopContainerRequest{\\n398: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n399: \\t\\tTimeout:     timeout,\\n400: \\t}); err != nil {\\n401: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"StopContainer from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n402: \\t\\treturn err\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopContainer Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n405: \\n406: \\treturn nil\\n407: }\\n408: \\n409: // RemoveContainer removes the container. If the container is running, the container\\n410: // should be forced to removal.\\n411: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RemoveContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\\n412: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemoveContainer\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n413: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n414: \\tdefer cancel()\\n415: \\n416: \\tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\\n417: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.RemoveContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.RemoveContainerRequest{\\n418: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n419: \\t}); err != nil {\\n420: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"RemoveContainer from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n421: \\t\\treturn err\\n422: \\t}\\n423: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemoveContainer Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n424: \\n425: \\treturn nil\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: // ListContainers lists containers by filters.\\n429: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListContainers(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Container, error) {\\n430: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainers\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n431: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n432: \\tdefer cancel()\\n433: \\n434: \\treturn r.listContainersV1(ctx, filter)\\n435: }\\n436: \\n437: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listContainersV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Container, error) {\\n438: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListContainers(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListContainersRequest{\\n439: \\t\\tFilter: filter,\\n440: \\t})\\n441: \\tif err != nil {\\n442: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListContainers with filter from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n443: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainers Response\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"containers\\\", resp.Containers)\\n446: \\n447: \\treturn resp.Containers, nil\\n448: }\\n449: \\n450: // ContainerStatus returns the container status.\\n451: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ContainerStatus(ctx context.Context, containerID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStatusResponse, error) {\\n452: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStatus\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n453: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n454: \\tdefer cancel()\\n455: \\n456: \\treturn r.containerStatusV1(ctx, containerID, verbose)\\n457: }\\n458: \\n459: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) containerStatusV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStatusResponse, error) {\\n460: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ContainerStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.ContainerStatusRequest{\\n461: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n462: \\t\\tVerbose:     verbose,\\n463: \\t})\\n464: \\tif err != nil {\\n465: \\t\\t// Don't spam the log with endless messages about the same failure.\\n466: \\t\\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), containerID) {\\n467: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ContainerStatus from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n468: \\t\\t}\\n469: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n470: \\t}\\n471: \\tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\\n472: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStatus Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"status\\\", resp.Status)\\n473: \\n474: \\tstatus := resp.Status\\n475: \\tif resp.Status != nil {\\n476: \\t\\tif err := verifyContainerStatus(status); err != nil {\\n477: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"verify ContainerStatus failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n478: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n479: \\t\\t}\\n480: \\t}\\n481: \\n482: \\treturn resp, nil\\n483: }\\n484: \\n485: // UpdateContainerResources updates a containers resource config\\n486: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, containerID string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) (err error) {\\n487: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateContainerResources\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n488: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n489: \\tdefer cancel()\\n490: \\n491: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdateContainerResources(ctx, &runtimeapi.UpdateContainerResourcesRequest{\\n492: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n493: \\t\\tLinux:       resources.GetLinux(),\\n494: \\t\\tWindows:     resources.GetWindows(),\\n495: \\t}); err != nil {\\n496: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"UpdateContainerResources from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n497: \\t\\treturn err\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateContainerResources Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n500: \\n501: \\treturn nil\\n502: }\\n503: \\n504: // ExecSync executes a command in the container, and returns the stdout output.\\n505: // If command exits with a non-zero exit code, an error is returned.\\n506: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ExecSync(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\\n507: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", timeout)\\n508: \\t// Do not set timeout when timeout is 0.\\n509: \\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\\n510: \\tif timeout != 0 {\\n511: \\t\\t// Use timeout + default timeout (2 minutes) as timeout to leave some time for\\n512: \\t\\t// the runtime to do cleanup.\\n513: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout+timeout)\\n514: \\t} else {\\n515: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\\n516: \\t}\\n517: \\tdefer cancel()\\n518: \\n519: \\treturn r.execSyncV1(ctx, containerID, cmd, timeout)\\n520: }\\n521: \\n522: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execSyncV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\\n523: \\ttimeoutSeconds := int64(timeout.Seconds())\\n524: \\treq := &runtimeapi.ExecSyncRequest{\\n525: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n526: \\t\\tCmd:         cmd,\\n527: \\t\\tTimeout:     timeoutSeconds,\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ExecSync(ctx, req)\\n530: \\tif err != nil {\\n531: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ExecSync cmd from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"cmd\\\", cmd)\\n532: \\n533: \\t\\t// interpret DeadlineExceeded gRPC errors as timedout errors\\n534: \\t\\tif status.Code(err) == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\\n535: \\t\\t\\terr = fmt.Errorf(\\\"%w: %q timed out after %s\\\", ErrCommandTimedOut, strings.Join(cmd, \\\" \\\"), timeout)\\n536: \\t\\t}\\n537: \\n538: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n539: \\t}\\n540: \\n541: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"exitCode\\\", resp.ExitCode)\\n542: \\terr = nil\\n543: \\tif resp.ExitCode != 0 {\\n544: \\t\\terr = utilexec.CodeExitError{\\n545: \\t\\t\\tErr:  fmt.Errorf(\\\"command '%s' exited with %d: %s\\\", strings.Join(cmd, \\\" \\\"), resp.ExitCode, resp.Stderr),\\n546: \\t\\t\\tCode: int(resp.ExitCode),\\n547: \\t\\t}\\n548: \\t}\\n549: \\n550: \\treturn resp.Stdout, resp.Stderr, err\\n551: }\\n552: \\n553: // Exec prepares a streaming endpoint to execute a command in the container, and returns the address.\\n554: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Exec(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.ExecRequest) (*runtimeapi.ExecResponse, error) {\\n555: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Exec\\\", \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n556: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n557: \\tdefer cancel()\\n558: \\n559: \\treturn r.execV1(ctx, req)\\n560: }\\n561: \\n562: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.ExecRequest) (*runtimeapi.ExecResponse, error) {\\n563: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Exec(ctx, req)\\n564: \\tif err != nil {\\n565: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Exec cmd from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", req.ContainerId, \\\"cmd\\\", req.Cmd)\\n566: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n567: \\t}\\n568: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Exec Response\\\")\\n569: \\n570: \\tif resp.Url == \\\"\\\" {\\n571: \\t\\terrorMessage := \\\"URL is not set\\\"\\n572: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n573: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Exec failed\\\")\\n574: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n575: \\t}\\n576: \\n577: \\treturn resp, nil\\n578: }\\n579: \\n580: // Attach prepares a streaming endpoint to attach to a running container, and returns the address.\\n581: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Attach(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.AttachRequest) (*runtimeapi.AttachResponse, error) {\\n582: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Attach\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", req.ContainerId, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n583: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n584: \\tdefer cancel()\\n585: \\n586: \\treturn r.attachV1(ctx, req)\\n587: }\\n588: \\n589: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) attachV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.AttachRequest) (*runtimeapi.AttachResponse, error) {\\n590: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Attach(ctx, req)\\n591: \\tif err != nil {\\n592: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Attach container from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", req.ContainerId)\\n593: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n594: \\t}\\n595: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Attach Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", req.ContainerId)\\n596: \\n597: \\tif resp.Url == \\\"\\\" {\\n598: \\t\\terrorMessage := \\\"URL is not set\\\"\\n599: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n600: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Attach failed\\\")\\n601: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n602: \\t}\\n603: \\treturn resp, nil\\n604: }\\n605: \\n606: // PortForward prepares a streaming endpoint to forward ports from a PodSandbox, and returns the address.\\n607: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PortForward(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.PortForwardRequest) (*runtimeapi.PortForwardResponse, error) {\\n608: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PortForward\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", req.PodSandboxId, \\\"port\\\", req.Port, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n609: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n610: \\tdefer cancel()\\n611: \\n612: \\treturn r.portForwardV1(ctx, req)\\n613: }\\n614: \\n615: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) portForwardV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.PortForwardRequest) (*runtimeapi.PortForwardResponse, error) {\\n616: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PortForward(ctx, req)\\n617: \\tif err != nil {\\n618: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"PortForward from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", req.PodSandboxId)\\n619: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n620: \\t}\\n621: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PortForward Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", req.PodSandboxId)\\n622: \\n623: \\tif resp.Url == \\\"\\\" {\\n624: \\t\\terrorMessage := \\\"URL is not set\\\"\\n625: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n626: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"PortForward failed\\\")\\n627: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n628: \\t}\\n629: \\n630: \\treturn resp, nil\\n631: }\\n632: \\n633: // UpdatePodSandboxResources synchronously updates the PodSandboxConfig with\\n634: // the pod-level resource configuration.\\n635: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdatePodSandboxResources(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.UpdatePodSandboxResourcesRequest) (*runtimeapi.UpdatePodSandboxResourcesResponse, error) {\\n636: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdatePodSandboxResources\\\", \\\"PodSandboxId\\\", req.PodSandboxId, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n637: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n638: \\tdefer cancel()\\n639: \\n640: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdatePodSandboxResources(ctx, req)\\n641: \\tif err != nil {\\n642: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"UpdatePodSandboxResources from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", req.PodSandboxId)\\n643: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n644: \\t}\\n645: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdatePodSandboxResources Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", req.PodSandboxId)\\n646: \\n647: \\treturn resp, nil\\n648: }\\n649: \\n650: // UpdateRuntimeConfig updates the config of a runtime service. The only\\n651: // update payload currently supported is the pod CIDR assigned to a node,\\n652: // and the runtime service just proxies it down to the network plugin.\\n653: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdateRuntimeConfig(ctx context.Context, runtimeConfig *runtimeapi.RuntimeConfig) (err error) {\\n654: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateRuntimeConfig\\\", \\\"runtimeConfig\\\", runtimeConfig, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n655: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n656: \\tdefer cancel()\\n657: \\n658: \\t// Response doesn't contain anything of interest. This translates to an\\n659: \\t// Event notification to the network plugin, which can't fail, so we're\\n660: \\t// really looking to surface destination unreachable.\\n661: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdateRuntimeConfig(ctx, &runtimeapi.UpdateRuntimeConfigRequest{\\n662: \\t\\tRuntimeConfig: runtimeConfig,\\n663: \\t}); err != nil {\\n664: \\t\\treturn err\\n665: \\t}\\n666: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateRuntimeConfig Response\\\", \\\"runtimeConfig\\\", runtimeConfig)\\n667: \\n668: \\treturn nil\\n669: }\\n670: \\n671: // Status returns the status of the runtime.\\n672: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Status(ctx context.Context, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.StatusResponse, error) {\\n673: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Status\\\", \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n674: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n675: \\tdefer cancel()\\n676: \\n677: \\treturn r.statusV1(ctx, verbose)\\n678: }\\n679: \\n680: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) statusV1(ctx context.Context, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.StatusResponse, error) {\\n681: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Status(ctx, &runtimeapi.StatusRequest{\\n682: \\t\\tVerbose: verbose,\\n683: \\t})\\n684: \\tif err != nil {\\n685: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Status from runtime service failed\\\")\\n686: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n687: \\t}\\n688: \\n689: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] Status Response\\\", \\\"status\\\", resp.Status)\\n690: \\n691: \\tif resp.Status == nil || len(resp.Status.Conditions) < 2 {\\n692: \\t\\terrorMessage := \\\"RuntimeReady or NetworkReady condition are not set\\\"\\n693: \\t\\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\\n694: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"Status failed\\\")\\n695: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n696: \\t}\\n697: \\n698: \\treturn resp, nil\\n699: }\\n700: \\n701: // ContainerStats returns the stats of the container.\\n702: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ContainerStats(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\\n703: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStats\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n704: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n705: \\tdefer cancel()\\n706: \\n707: \\treturn r.containerStatsV1(ctx, containerID)\\n708: }\\n709: \\n710: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) containerStatsV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\\n711: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ContainerStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ContainerStatsRequest{\\n712: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n713: \\t})\\n714: \\tif err != nil {\\n715: \\t\\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), containerID) {\\n716: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ContainerStats from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n717: \\t\\t}\\n718: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n719: \\t}\\n720: \\tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\\n721: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStats Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetStats())\\n722: \\n723: \\treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\\n724: }\\n725: \\n726: // ListContainerStats returns the list of ContainerStats given the filter.\\n727: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListContainerStats(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\\n728: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainerStats\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n729: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n730: \\tdefer cancel()\\n731: \\n732: \\treturn r.listContainerStatsV1(ctx, filter)\\n733: }\\n734: \\n735: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listContainerStatsV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\\n736: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListContainerStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListContainerStatsRequest{\\n737: \\t\\tFilter: filter,\\n738: \\t})\\n739: \\tif err != nil {\\n740: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListContainerStats with filter from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n741: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n742: \\t}\\n743: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainerStats Response\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetStats())\\n744: \\n745: \\treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\\n746: }\\n747: \\n748: // PodSandboxStats returns the stats of the pod.\\n749: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PodSandboxStats(ctx context.Context, podSandboxID string) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\\n750: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStats\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandboxID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n751: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n752: \\tdefer cancel()\\n753: \\n754: \\treturn r.podSandboxStatsV1(ctx, podSandboxID)\\n755: }\\n756: \\n757: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) podSandboxStatsV1(ctx context.Context, podSandboxID string) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\\n758: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PodSandboxStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsRequest{\\n759: \\t\\tPodSandboxId: podSandboxID,\\n760: \\t})\\n761: \\tif err != nil {\\n762: \\t\\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), podSandboxID) {\\n763: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"PodSandbox from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandboxID)\\n764: \\t\\t}\\n765: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n766: \\t}\\n767: \\tr.logReduction.ClearID(podSandboxID)\\n768: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandbox Response\\\", \\\"podSandboxID\\\", podSandboxID, \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetStats())\\n769: \\n770: \\treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\\n771: }\\n772: \\n773: // ListPodSandboxStats returns the list of pod sandbox stats given the filter\\n774: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListPodSandboxStats(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\\n775: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandboxStats\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n776: \\t// Set timeout, because runtimes are able to cache disk stats results\\n777: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n778: \\tdefer cancel()\\n779: \\n780: \\treturn r.listPodSandboxStatsV1(ctx, filter)\\n781: }\\n782: \\n783: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listPodSandboxStatsV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\\n784: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListPodSandboxStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListPodSandboxStatsRequest{\\n785: \\t\\tFilter: filter,\\n786: \\t})\\n787: \\tif err != nil {\\n788: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListPodSandboxStats with filter from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter)\\n789: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n790: \\t}\\n791: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandboxStats Response\\\", \\\"filter\\\", filter, \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetStats())\\n792: \\n793: \\treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\\n794: }\\n795: \\n796: // ReopenContainerLog reopens the container log file.\\n797: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ReopenContainerLog(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\\n798: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ReopenContainerLog\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", r.timeout)\\n799: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n800: \\tdefer cancel()\\n801: \\n802: \\tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.ReopenContainerLog(ctx, &runtimeapi.ReopenContainerLogRequest{ContainerId: containerID}); err != nil {\\n803: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ReopenContainerLog from runtime service failed\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n804: \\t\\treturn err\\n805: \\t}\\n806: \\n807: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ReopenContainerLog Response\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n808: \\treturn nil\\n809: }\\n810: \\n811: // CheckpointContainer triggers a checkpoint of the given CheckpointContainerRequest\\n812: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) CheckpointContainer(ctx context.Context, options *runtimeapi.CheckpointContainerRequest) error {\\n813: \\tr.log(10,\\n814: \\t\\t\\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CheckpointContainer\\\",\\n815: \\t\\t\\\"options\\\",\\n816: \\t\\toptions,\\n817: \\t)\\n818: \\tif options == nil {\\n819: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"CheckpointContainer requires non-nil CheckpointRestoreOptions parameter\\\")\\n820: \\t}\\n821: \\tif options.Timeout < 0 {\\n822: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"CheckpointContainer requires the timeout value to be > 0\\\")\\n823: \\t}\\n824: \\n825: \\tctx, cancel := func(ctx context.Context) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\\n826: \\t\\tdefaultTimeout := int64(r.timeout / time.Second)\\n827: \\t\\tif options.Timeout > defaultTimeout {\\n828: \\t\\t\\t// The user requested a specific timeout, let's use that if it\\n829: \\t\\t\\t// is larger than the CRI default.\\n830: \\t\\t\\treturn context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(options.Timeout)*time.Second)\\n831: \\t\\t}\\n832: \\t\\t// If the user requested a timeout less than the\\n833: \\t\\t// CRI default, let's use the CRI default.\\n834: \\t\\toptions.Timeout = defaultTimeout\\n835: \\t\\treturn context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n836: \\t}(ctx)\\n837: \\tdefer cancel()\\n838: \\n839: \\t_, err := r.runtimeClient.CheckpointContainer(\\n840: \\t\\tctx,\\n841: \\t\\toptions,\\n842: \\t)\\n843: \\n844: \\tif err != nil {\\n845: \\t\\tr.logErr(\\n846: \\t\\t\\terr,\\n847: \\t\\t\\t\\\"CheckpointContainer from runtime service failed\\\",\\n848: \\t\\t\\t\\\"containerID\\\",\\n849: \\t\\t\\toptions.ContainerId,\\n850: \\t\\t)\\n851: \\t\\treturn err\\n852: \\t}\\n853: \\tr.log(10,\\n854: \\t\\t\\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CheckpointContainer Response\\\",\\n855: \\t\\t\\\"containerID\\\",\\n856: \\t\\toptions.ContainerId,\\n857: \\t)\\n858: \\n859: \\treturn nil\\n860: }\\n861: \\n862: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) GetContainerEvents(ctx context.Context, containerEventsCh chan *runtimeapi.ContainerEventResponse, connectionEstablishedCallback func(runtimeapi.RuntimeService_GetContainerEventsClient)) error {\\n863: \\tcontainerEventsStreamingClient, err := r.runtimeClient.GetContainerEvents(ctx, &runtimeapi.GetEventsRequest{})\\n864: \\tif err != nil {\\n865: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"GetContainerEvents failed to get streaming client\\\")\\n866: \\t\\treturn err\\n867: \\t}\\n868: \\n869: \\tif connectionEstablishedCallback != nil {\\n870: \\t\\t// The connection is successfully established and we have a streaming client ready for use.\\n871: \\t\\tconnectionEstablishedCallback(containerEventsStreamingClient)\\n872: \\t}\\n873: \\n874: \\tfor {\\n875: \\t\\tresp, err := containerEventsStreamingClient.Recv()\\n876: \\t\\tif err == io.EOF {\\n877: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"container events stream is closed\\\")\\n878: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n879: \\t\\t}\\n880: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n881: \\t\\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"failed to receive streaming container event\\\")\\n882: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n883: \\t\\t}\\n884: \\t\\tif resp != nil {\\n885: \\t\\t\\tcontainerEventsCh <- resp\\n886: \\t\\t\\tr.log(4, \\\"container event received\\\", \\\"resp\\\", resp)\\n887: \\t\\t}\\n888: \\t}\\n889: }\\n890: \\n891: // ListMetricDescriptors gets the descriptors for the metrics that will be returned in ListPodSandboxMetrics.\\n892: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListMetricDescriptors(ctx context.Context) ([]*runtimeapi.MetricDescriptor, error) {\\n893: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n894: \\tdefer cancel()\\n895: \\n896: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListMetricDescriptors(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListMetricDescriptorsRequest{})\\n897: \\tif err != nil {\\n898: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListMetricDescriptors from runtime service failed\\\")\\n899: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n900: \\t}\\n901: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListMetricDescriptors Response\\\", \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetDescriptors())\\n902: \\n903: \\treturn resp.GetDescriptors(), nil\\n904: }\\n905: \\n906: // ListPodSandboxMetrics retrieves the metrics for all pod sandboxes.\\n907: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListPodSandboxMetrics(ctx context.Context) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandboxMetrics, error) {\\n908: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n909: \\tdefer cancel()\\n910: \\n911: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListPodSandboxMetrics(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListPodSandboxMetricsRequest{})\\n912: \\tif err != nil {\\n913: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"ListPodSandboxMetrics from runtime service failed\\\")\\n914: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n915: \\t}\\n916: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandboxMetrics Response\\\", \\\"stats\\\", resp.GetPodMetrics())\\n917: \\n918: \\treturn resp.GetPodMetrics(), nil\\n919: }\\n920: \\n921: // RuntimeConfig returns the configuration information of the runtime.\\n922: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RuntimeConfig(ctx context.Context) (*runtimeapi.RuntimeConfigResponse, error) {\\n923: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\\n924: \\tdefer cancel()\\n925: \\n926: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.RuntimeConfig(ctx, &runtimeapi.RuntimeConfigRequest{})\\n927: \\tif err != nil {\\n928: \\t\\tr.logErr(err, \\\"RuntimeConfig from runtime service failed\\\")\\n929: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n930: \\t}\\n931: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] RuntimeConfigResponse\\\", \\\"linuxConfig\\\", resp.GetLinux())\\n932: \\n933: \\treturn resp, nil\\n934: }\\n935: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--8c9c67c0@8c9c67c000104450cfc5a5f48053a9a84b73cf93/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/remote_runtime.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package cri\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"io\"\n24: \t\"strings\"\n25: \t\"time\"\n26: \n27: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/google.golang.org/grpc/otelgrpc\"\n28: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n30: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/backoff\"\n31: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n32: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\"\n33: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n34: \n35: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs/logreduction\"\n36: \ttracing \"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n37: \tinternalapi \"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis\"\n38: \truntimeapi \"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\"\n39: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n40: \tutilexec \"k8s.io/utils/exec\"\n41: \n42: \t\"k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/internal\"\n43: \t\"k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/util\"\n44: )\n45: \n46: // remoteRuntimeService is a gRPC implementation of internalapi.RuntimeService.\n47: type remoteRuntimeService struct {\n48: \ttimeout       time.Duration\n49: \truntimeClient runtimeapi.RuntimeServiceClient\n50: \t// Cache last per-container error message to reduce log spam\n51: \tlogReduction *logreduction.LogReduction\n52: \tlogger       *klog.Logger\n53: \tconn         *grpc.ClientConn\n54: }\n55: \n56: const (\n57: \t// How frequently to report identical errors\n58: \tidenticalErrorDelay = 1 * time.Minute\n59: \n60: \t// connection parameters\n61: \tmaxBackoffDelay      = 3 * time.Second\n62: \tbaseBackoffDelay     = 100 * time.Millisecond\n63: \tminConnectionTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n64: )\n65: \n66: // CRIVersion is the type for valid Container Runtime Interface (CRI) API\n67: // versions.\n68: type CRIVersion string\n69: \n70: var (\n71: \t// ErrContainerStatusNil indicates that the returned container status is nil.\n72: \tErrContainerStatusNil = errors.New(\"container status is nil\")\n73: \n74: \t// ErrCommandTimedOut indicates that the exec sync command timed.\n75: \tErrCommandTimedOut = errors.New(\"command timed out\")\n76: )\n77: \n78: const (\n79: \t// CRIVersionV1 references the v1 CRI API.\n80: \tCRIVersionV1 CRIVersion = \"v1\"\n81: )\n82: \n83: // NewRemoteRuntimeService creates a new internalapi.RuntimeService.\n84: func NewRemoteRuntimeService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.RuntimeService, error) {\n85: \tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \"Connecting to runtime service\", \"endpoint\", endpoint)\n86: \taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\n87: \tif err != nil {\n88: \t\treturn nil, err\n89: \t}\n90: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\n91: \tdefer cancel()\n92: \n93: \tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\n94: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n95: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n96: \t\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\"localhost\"),\n97: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\n98: \t\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\n99: \tif tp != nil {\n100: \t\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\n101: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\n102: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\n103: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\n104: \t\t}\n105: \t\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\n106: \t\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\n107: \t\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n108: \t\t\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\n109: \t}\n110: \n111: \tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\n112: \t\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\n113: \t}\n114: \tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\n115: \tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\n116: \tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\n117: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n118: \t\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\n119: \t)\n120: \n121: \tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\n122: \tif err != nil {\n123: \t\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \"Connect remote runtime failed\", \"address\", addr)\n124: \t\treturn nil, err\n125: \t}\n126: \n127: \tservice := &remoteRuntimeService{\n128: \t\ttimeout:      connectionTimeout,\n129: \t\tlogReduction: logreduction.NewLogReduction(identicalErrorDelay),\n130: \t\tlogger:       logger,\n131: \t\tconn:         conn,\n132: \t}\n133: \n134: \tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\n135: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"validate service connection: %w\", err)\n136: \t}\n137: \n138: \treturn service, nil\n139: }\n140: \n141: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\n142: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Close() error {\n143: \tr.log(3, \"Closing runtime service connection\")\n144: \treturn r.conn.Close()\n145: }\n146: \n147: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n148: \tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\n149: }\n150: \n151: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n152: \tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\n153: }\n154: \n155: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote runtime service by\n156: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\n157: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\n158: \tr.log(4, \"Validating the CRI v1 API runtime version\")\n159: \tr.runtimeClient = runtimeapi.NewRuntimeServiceClient(conn)\n160: \n161: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.Version(ctx, &runtimeapi.VersionRequest{}); err != nil {\n162: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"validate CRI v1 runtime API for endpoint %q: %w\", endpoint, err)\n163: \t}\n164: \n165: \tr.log(2, \"Validated CRI v1 runtime API\")\n166: \treturn nil\n167: }\n168: \n169: // Version returns the runtime name, runtime version and runtime API version.\n170: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Version(ctx context.Context, apiVersion string) (*runtimeapi.VersionResponse, error) {\n171: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Version\", \"apiVersion\", apiVersion, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n172: \n173: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n174: \tdefer cancel()\n175: \n176: \treturn r.versionV1(ctx, apiVersion)\n177: }\n178: \n179: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) versionV1(ctx context.Context, apiVersion string) (*runtimeapi.VersionResponse, error) {\n180: \ttypedVersion, err := r.runtimeClient.Version(ctx, &runtimeapi.VersionRequest{\n181: \t\tVersion: apiVersion,\n182: \t})\n183: \tif err != nil {\n184: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Version from runtime service failed\")\n185: \t\treturn nil, err\n186: \t}\n187: \n188: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Version Response\", \"apiVersion\", typedVersion)\n189: \n190: \tvar missingFields []string\n191: \tif typedVersion.Version == \"\" {\n192: \t\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \"Version\")\n193: \t}\n194: \tif typedVersion.RuntimeName == \"\" {\n195: \t\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \"RuntimeName\")\n196: \t}\n197: \tif typedVersion.RuntimeApiVersion == \"\" {\n198: \t\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \"RuntimeApiVersion\")\n199: \t}\n200: \tif typedVersion.RuntimeVersion == \"\" {\n201: \t\tmissingFields = append(missingFields, \"RuntimeVersion\")\n202: \t}\n203: \tif len(missingFields) > 0 {\n204: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"not all fields are set in VersionResponse (missing %s)\", strings.Join(missingFields, \", \"))\n205: \t}\n206: \n207: \treturn typedVersion, err\n208: }\n209: \n210: // RunPodSandbox creates and starts a pod-level sandbox. Runtimes should ensure\n211: // the sandbox is in ready state.\n212: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RunPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, config *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig, runtimeHandler string) (string, error) {\n213: \t// Use 2 times longer timeout for sandbox operation (4 mins by default)\n214: \t// TODO: Make the pod sandbox timeout configurable.\n215: \ttimeout := r.timeout * 2\n216: \n217: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RunPodSandbox\", \"config\", config, \"runtimeHandler\", runtimeHandler, \"timeout\", timeout)\n218: \n219: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n220: \tdefer cancel()\n221: \n222: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.RunPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.RunPodSandboxRequest{\n223: \t\tConfig:         config,\n224: \t\tRuntimeHandler: runtimeHandler,\n225: \t})\n226: \n227: \tif err != nil {\n228: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"RunPodSandbox from runtime service failed\")\n229: \t\treturn \"\", err\n230: \t}\n231: \n232: \tpodSandboxID := resp.PodSandboxId\n233: \n234: \tif podSandboxID == \"\" {\n235: \t\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\"PodSandboxId is not set for sandbox %q\", config.Metadata)\n236: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n237: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"RunPodSandbox failed\")\n238: \t\treturn \"\", err\n239: \t}\n240: \n241: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RunPodSandbox Response\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandboxID)\n242: \n243: \treturn podSandboxID, nil\n244: }\n245: \n246: // StopPodSandbox stops the sandbox. If there are any running containers in the\n247: // sandbox, they should be forced to termination.\n248: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StopPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string) (err error) {\n249: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopPodSandbox\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n250: \n251: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n252: \tdefer cancel()\n253: \n254: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StopPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.StopPodSandboxRequest{\n255: \t\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\n256: \t}); err != nil {\n257: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"StopPodSandbox from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID)\n258: \t\treturn err\n259: \t}\n260: \n261: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopPodSandbox Response\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID)\n262: \n263: \treturn nil\n264: }\n265: \n266: // RemovePodSandbox removes the sandbox. If there are any containers in the\n267: // sandbox, they should be forcibly removed.\n268: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RemovePodSandbox(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string) (err error) {\n269: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemovePodSandbox\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n270: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n271: \tdefer cancel()\n272: \n273: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.RemovePodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.RemovePodSandboxRequest{\n274: \t\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\n275: \t}); err != nil {\n276: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"RemovePodSandbox from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID)\n277: \t\treturn err\n278: \t}\n279: \n280: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemovePodSandbox Response\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID)\n281: \n282: \treturn nil\n283: }\n284: \n285: // PodSandboxStatus returns the status of the PodSandbox.\n286: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PodSandboxStatus(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusResponse, error) {\n287: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStatus\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n288: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n289: \tdefer cancel()\n290: \n291: \treturn r.podSandboxStatusV1(ctx, podSandBoxID, verbose)\n292: }\n293: \n294: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) podSandboxStatusV1(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusResponse, error) {\n295: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PodSandboxStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatusRequest{\n296: \t\tPodSandboxId: podSandBoxID,\n297: \t\tVerbose:      verbose,\n298: \t})\n299: \tif err != nil {\n300: \t\treturn nil, err\n301: \t}\n302: \n303: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStatus Response\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"status\", resp.Status)\n304: \n305: \tstatus := resp.Status\n306: \tif resp.Status != nil {\n307: \t\tif err := verifySandboxStatus(status); err != nil {\n308: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n309: \t\t}\n310: \t}\n311: \n312: \treturn resp, nil\n313: }\n314: \n315: // ListPodSandbox returns a list of PodSandboxes.\n316: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListPodSandbox(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandbox, error) {\n317: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandbox\", \"filter\", filter, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n318: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n319: \tdefer cancel()\n320: \n321: \treturn r.listPodSandboxV1(ctx, filter)\n322: }\n323: \n324: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listPodSandboxV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandbox, error) {\n325: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListPodSandbox(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListPodSandboxRequest{\n326: \t\tFilter: filter,\n327: \t})\n328: \tif err != nil {\n329: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ListPodSandbox with filter from runtime service failed\", \"filter\", filter)\n330: \t\treturn nil, err\n331: \t}\n332: \n333: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandbox Response\", \"filter\", filter, \"items\", resp.Items)\n334: \n335: \treturn resp.Items, nil\n336: }\n337: \n338: // CreateContainer creates a new container in the specified PodSandbox.\n339: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) CreateContainer(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, config *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig, sandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\n340: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] CreateContainer\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n341: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n342: \tdefer cancel()\n343: \n344: \treturn r.createContainerV1(ctx, podSandBoxID, config, sandboxConfig)\n345: }\n346: \n347: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) createContainerV1(ctx context.Context, podSandBoxID string, config *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig, sandboxConfig *runtimeapi.PodSandboxConfig) (string, error) {\n348: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.CreateContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.CreateContainerRequest{\n349: \t\tPodSandboxId:  podSandBoxID,\n350: \t\tConfig:        config,\n351: \t\tSandboxConfig: sandboxConfig,\n352: \t})\n353: \tif err != nil {\n354: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"CreateContainer in sandbox from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID)\n355: \t\treturn \"\", err\n356: \t}\n357: \n358: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] CreateContainer\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandBoxID, \"containerID\", resp.ContainerId)\n359: \tif resp.ContainerId == \"\" {\n360: \t\terrorMessage := fmt.Sprintf(\"ContainerId is not set for container %q\", config.Metadata)\n361: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n362: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"CreateContainer failed\")\n363: \t\treturn \"\", err\n364: \t}\n365: \n366: \treturn resp.ContainerId, nil\n367: }\n368: \n369: // StartContainer starts the container.\n370: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StartContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\n371: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StartContainer\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n372: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n373: \tdefer cancel()\n374: \n375: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StartContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.StartContainerRequest{\n376: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n377: \t}); err != nil {\n378: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"StartContainer from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n379: \t\treturn err\n380: \t}\n381: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StartContainer Response\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n382: \n383: \treturn nil\n384: }\n385: \n386: // StopContainer stops a running container with a grace period (i.e., timeout).\n387: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) StopContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string, timeout int64) (err error) {\n388: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopContainer\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", timeout)\n389: \t// Use timeout + default timeout (2 minutes) as timeout to leave extra time\n390: \t// for SIGKILL container and request latency.\n391: \tt := r.timeout + time.Duration(timeout)*time.Second\n392: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, t)\n393: \tdefer cancel()\n394: \n395: \tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\n396: \n397: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.StopContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.StopContainerRequest{\n398: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n399: \t\tTimeout:     timeout,\n400: \t}); err != nil {\n401: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"StopContainer from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n402: \t\treturn err\n403: \t}\n404: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] StopContainer Response\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n405: \n406: \treturn nil\n407: }\n408: \n409: // RemoveContainer removes the container. If the container is running, the container\n410: // should be forced to removal.\n411: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) RemoveContainer(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\n412: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemoveContainer\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n413: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n414: \tdefer cancel()\n415: \n416: \tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\n417: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.RemoveContainer(ctx, &runtimeapi.RemoveContainerRequest{\n418: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n419: \t}); err != nil {\n420: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"RemoveContainer from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n421: \t\treturn err\n422: \t}\n423: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] RemoveContainer Response\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n424: \n425: \treturn nil\n426: }\n427: \n428: // ListContainers lists containers by filters.\n429: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListContainers(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Container, error) {\n430: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainers\", \"filter\", filter, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n431: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n432: \tdefer cancel()\n433: \n434: \treturn r.listContainersV1(ctx, filter)\n435: }\n436: \n437: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listContainersV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.Container, error) {\n438: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListContainers(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListContainersRequest{\n439: \t\tFilter: filter,\n440: \t})\n441: \tif err != nil {\n442: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ListContainers with filter from runtime service failed\", \"filter\", filter)\n443: \t\treturn nil, err\n444: \t}\n445: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainers Response\", \"filter\", filter, \"containers\", resp.Containers)\n446: \n447: \treturn resp.Containers, nil\n448: }\n449: \n450: // ContainerStatus returns the container status.\n451: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ContainerStatus(ctx context.Context, containerID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStatusResponse, error) {\n452: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStatus\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n453: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n454: \tdefer cancel()\n455: \n456: \treturn r.containerStatusV1(ctx, containerID, verbose)\n457: }\n458: \n459: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) containerStatusV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStatusResponse, error) {\n460: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ContainerStatus(ctx, &runtimeapi.ContainerStatusRequest{\n461: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n462: \t\tVerbose:     verbose,\n463: \t})\n464: \tif err != nil {\n465: \t\t// Don't spam the log with endless messages about the same failure.\n466: \t\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), containerID) {\n467: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"ContainerStatus from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n468: \t\t}\n469: \t\treturn nil, err\n470: \t}\n471: \tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\n472: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStatus Response\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"status\", resp.Status)\n473: \n474: \tstatus := resp.Status\n475: \tif resp.Status != nil {\n476: \t\tif err := verifyContainerStatus(status); err != nil {\n477: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"verify ContainerStatus failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n478: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n479: \t\t}\n480: \t}\n481: \n482: \treturn resp, nil\n483: }\n484: \n485: // UpdateContainerResources updates a containers resource config\n486: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, containerID string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) (err error) {\n487: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateContainerResources\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n488: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n489: \tdefer cancel()\n490: \n491: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdateContainerResources(ctx, &runtimeapi.UpdateContainerResourcesRequest{\n492: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n493: \t\tLinux:       resources.GetLinux(),\n494: \t\tWindows:     resources.GetWindows(),\n495: \t}); err != nil {\n496: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"UpdateContainerResources from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n497: \t\treturn err\n498: \t}\n499: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateContainerResources Response\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n500: \n501: \treturn nil\n502: }\n503: \n504: // ExecSync executes a command in the container, and returns the stdout output.\n505: // If command exits with a non-zero exit code, an error is returned.\n506: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ExecSync(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\n507: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", timeout)\n508: \t// Do not set timeout when timeout is 0.\n509: \tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n510: \tif timeout != 0 {\n511: \t\t// Use timeout + default timeout (2 minutes) as timeout to leave some time for\n512: \t\t// the runtime to do cleanup.\n513: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout+timeout)\n514: \t} else {\n515: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n516: \t}\n517: \tdefer cancel()\n518: \n519: \treturn r.execSyncV1(ctx, containerID, cmd, timeout)\n520: }\n521: \n522: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execSyncV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\n523: \ttimeoutSeconds := int64(timeout.Seconds())\n524: \treq := &runtimeapi.ExecSyncRequest{\n525: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n526: \t\tCmd:         cmd,\n527: \t\tTimeout:     timeoutSeconds,\n528: \t}\n529: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ExecSync(ctx, req)\n530: \tif err != nil {\n531: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ExecSync cmd from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"cmd\", cmd)\n532: \n533: \t\t// interpret DeadlineExceeded gRPC errors as timedout errors\n534: \t\tif status.Code(err) == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\n535: \t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"%w: %q timed out after %s\", ErrCommandTimedOut, strings.Join(cmd, \" \"), timeout)\n536: \t\t}\n537: \n538: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n539: \t}\n540: \n541: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync Response\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"exitCode\", resp.ExitCode)\n542: \terr = nil\n543: \tif resp.ExitCode != 0 {\n544: \t\terr = utilexec.CodeExitError{\n545: \t\t\tErr:  fmt.Errorf(\"command '%s' exited with %d: %s\", strings.Join(cmd, \" \"), resp.ExitCode, resp.Stderr),\n546: \t\t\tCode: int(resp.ExitCode),\n547: \t\t}\n548: \t}\n549: \n550: \treturn resp.Stdout, resp.Stderr, err\n551: }\n552: \n553: // Exec prepares a streaming endpoint to execute a command in the container, and returns the address.\n554: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Exec(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.ExecRequest) (*runtimeapi.ExecResponse, error) {\n555: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Exec\", \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n556: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n557: \tdefer cancel()\n558: \n559: \treturn r.execV1(ctx, req)\n560: }\n561: \n562: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.ExecRequest) (*runtimeapi.ExecResponse, error) {\n563: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Exec(ctx, req)\n564: \tif err != nil {\n565: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Exec cmd from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", req.ContainerId, \"cmd\", req.Cmd)\n566: \t\treturn nil, err\n567: \t}\n568: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Exec Response\")\n569: \n570: \tif resp.Url == \"\" {\n571: \t\terrorMessage := \"URL is not set\"\n572: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n573: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Exec failed\")\n574: \t\treturn nil, err\n575: \t}\n576: \n577: \treturn resp, nil\n578: }\n579: \n580: // Attach prepares a streaming endpoint to attach to a running container, and returns the address.\n581: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Attach(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.AttachRequest) (*runtimeapi.AttachResponse, error) {\n582: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Attach\", \"containerID\", req.ContainerId, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n583: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n584: \tdefer cancel()\n585: \n586: \treturn r.attachV1(ctx, req)\n587: }\n588: \n589: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) attachV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.AttachRequest) (*runtimeapi.AttachResponse, error) {\n590: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Attach(ctx, req)\n591: \tif err != nil {\n592: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Attach container from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", req.ContainerId)\n593: \t\treturn nil, err\n594: \t}\n595: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Attach Response\", \"containerID\", req.ContainerId)\n596: \n597: \tif resp.Url == \"\" {\n598: \t\terrorMessage := \"URL is not set\"\n599: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n600: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Attach failed\")\n601: \t\treturn nil, err\n602: \t}\n603: \treturn resp, nil\n604: }\n605: \n606: // PortForward prepares a streaming endpoint to forward ports from a PodSandbox, and returns the address.\n607: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PortForward(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.PortForwardRequest) (*runtimeapi.PortForwardResponse, error) {\n608: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PortForward\", \"podSandboxID\", req.PodSandboxId, \"port\", req.Port, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n609: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n610: \tdefer cancel()\n611: \n612: \treturn r.portForwardV1(ctx, req)\n613: }\n614: \n615: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) portForwardV1(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.PortForwardRequest) (*runtimeapi.PortForwardResponse, error) {\n616: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PortForward(ctx, req)\n617: \tif err != nil {\n618: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"PortForward from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", req.PodSandboxId)\n619: \t\treturn nil, err\n620: \t}\n621: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PortForward Response\", \"podSandboxID\", req.PodSandboxId)\n622: \n623: \tif resp.Url == \"\" {\n624: \t\terrorMessage := \"URL is not set\"\n625: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n626: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"PortForward failed\")\n627: \t\treturn nil, err\n628: \t}\n629: \n630: \treturn resp, nil\n631: }\n632: \n633: // UpdatePodSandboxResources synchronously updates the PodSandboxConfig with\n634: // the pod-level resource configuration.\n635: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdatePodSandboxResources(ctx context.Context, req *runtimeapi.UpdatePodSandboxResourcesRequest) (*runtimeapi.UpdatePodSandboxResourcesResponse, error) {\n636: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdatePodSandboxResources\", \"PodSandboxId\", req.PodSandboxId, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n637: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n638: \tdefer cancel()\n639: \n640: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdatePodSandboxResources(ctx, req)\n641: \tif err != nil {\n642: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"UpdatePodSandboxResources from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", req.PodSandboxId)\n643: \t\treturn nil, err\n644: \t}\n645: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdatePodSandboxResources Response\", \"podSandboxID\", req.PodSandboxId)\n646: \n647: \treturn resp, nil\n648: }\n649: \n650: // UpdateRuntimeConfig updates the config of a runtime service. The only\n651: // update payload currently supported is the pod CIDR assigned to a node,\n652: // and the runtime service just proxies it down to the network plugin.\n653: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) UpdateRuntimeConfig(ctx context.Context, runtimeConfig *runtimeapi.RuntimeConfig) (err error) {\n654: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateRuntimeConfig\", \"runtimeConfig\", runtimeConfig, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n655: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n656: \tdefer cancel()\n657: \n658: \t// Response doesn't contain anything of interest. This translates to an\n659: \t// Event notification to the network plugin, which can't fail, so we're\n660: \t// really looking to surface destination unreachable.\n661: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.UpdateRuntimeConfig(ctx, &runtimeapi.UpdateRuntimeConfigRequest{\n662: \t\tRuntimeConfig: runtimeConfig,\n663: \t}); err != nil {\n664: \t\treturn err\n665: \t}\n666: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] UpdateRuntimeConfig Response\", \"runtimeConfig\", runtimeConfig)\n667: \n668: \treturn nil\n669: }\n670: \n671: // Status returns the status of the runtime.\n672: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Status(ctx context.Context, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.StatusResponse, error) {\n673: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Status\", \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n674: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n675: \tdefer cancel()\n676: \n677: \treturn r.statusV1(ctx, verbose)\n678: }\n679: \n680: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) statusV1(ctx context.Context, verbose bool) (*runtimeapi.StatusResponse, error) {\n681: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.Status(ctx, &runtimeapi.StatusRequest{\n682: \t\tVerbose: verbose,\n683: \t})\n684: \tif err != nil {\n685: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Status from runtime service failed\")\n686: \t\treturn nil, err\n687: \t}\n688: \n689: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] Status Response\", \"status\", resp.Status)\n690: \n691: \tif resp.Status == nil || len(resp.Status.Conditions) < 2 {\n692: \t\terrorMessage := \"RuntimeReady or NetworkReady condition are not set\"\n693: \t\terr := errors.New(errorMessage)\n694: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"Status failed\")\n695: \t\treturn nil, err\n696: \t}\n697: \n698: \treturn resp, nil\n699: }\n700: \n701: // ContainerStats returns the stats of the container.\n702: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ContainerStats(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\n703: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStats\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n704: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n705: \tdefer cancel()\n706: \n707: \treturn r.containerStatsV1(ctx, containerID)\n708: }\n709: \n710: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) containerStatsV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\n711: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ContainerStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ContainerStatsRequest{\n712: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n713: \t})\n714: \tif err != nil {\n715: \t\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), containerID) {\n716: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"ContainerStats from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n717: \t\t}\n718: \t\treturn nil, err\n719: \t}\n720: \tr.logReduction.ClearID(containerID)\n721: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ContainerStats Response\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"stats\", resp.GetStats())\n722: \n723: \treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\n724: }\n725: \n726: // ListContainerStats returns the list of ContainerStats given the filter.\n727: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListContainerStats(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\n728: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainerStats\", \"filter\", filter)\n729: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n730: \tdefer cancel()\n731: \n732: \treturn r.listContainerStatsV1(ctx, filter)\n733: }\n734: \n735: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listContainerStatsV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.ContainerStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.ContainerStats, error) {\n736: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListContainerStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListContainerStatsRequest{\n737: \t\tFilter: filter,\n738: \t})\n739: \tif err != nil {\n740: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ListContainerStats with filter from runtime service failed\", \"filter\", filter)\n741: \t\treturn nil, err\n742: \t}\n743: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListContainerStats Response\", \"filter\", filter, \"stats\", resp.GetStats())\n744: \n745: \treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\n746: }\n747: \n748: // PodSandboxStats returns the stats of the pod.\n749: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) PodSandboxStats(ctx context.Context, podSandboxID string) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\n750: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandboxStats\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandboxID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n751: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n752: \tdefer cancel()\n753: \n754: \treturn r.podSandboxStatsV1(ctx, podSandboxID)\n755: }\n756: \n757: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) podSandboxStatsV1(ctx context.Context, podSandboxID string) (*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\n758: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.PodSandboxStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsRequest{\n759: \t\tPodSandboxId: podSandboxID,\n760: \t})\n761: \tif err != nil {\n762: \t\tif r.logReduction.ShouldMessageBePrinted(err.Error(), podSandboxID) {\n763: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"PodSandbox from runtime service failed\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandboxID)\n764: \t\t}\n765: \t\treturn nil, err\n766: \t}\n767: \tr.logReduction.ClearID(podSandboxID)\n768: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] PodSandbox Response\", \"podSandboxID\", podSandboxID, \"stats\", resp.GetStats())\n769: \n770: \treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\n771: }\n772: \n773: // ListPodSandboxStats returns the list of pod sandbox stats given the filter\n774: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListPodSandboxStats(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\n775: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandboxStats\", \"filter\", filter)\n776: \t// Set timeout, because runtimes are able to cache disk stats results\n777: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n778: \tdefer cancel()\n779: \n780: \treturn r.listPodSandboxStatsV1(ctx, filter)\n781: }\n782: \n783: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) listPodSandboxStatsV1(ctx context.Context, filter *runtimeapi.PodSandboxStatsFilter) ([]*runtimeapi.PodSandboxStats, error) {\n784: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ListPodSandboxStats(ctx, &runtimeapi.ListPodSandboxStatsRequest{\n785: \t\tFilter: filter,\n786: \t})\n787: \tif err != nil {\n788: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ListPodSandboxStats with filter from runtime service failed\", \"filter\", filter)\n789: \t\treturn nil, err\n790: \t}\n791: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ListPodSandboxStats Response\", \"filter\", filter, \"stats\", resp.GetStats())\n792: \n793: \treturn resp.GetStats(), nil\n794: }\n795: \n796: // ReopenContainerLog reopens the container log file.\n797: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ReopenContainerLog(ctx context.Context, containerID string) (err error) {\n798: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ReopenContainerLog\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", r.timeout)\n799: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n800: \tdefer cancel()\n801: \n802: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.ReopenContainerLog(ctx, &runtimeapi.ReopenContainerLogRequest{ContainerId: containerID}); err != nil {\n803: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ReopenContainerLog from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n804: \t\treturn err\n805: \t}\n806: \n807: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ReopenContainerLog Response\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n808: \treturn nil\n809: }\n810: \n811: // CheckpointContainer triggers a checkpoint of the given CheckpointContainerRequest\n812: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) CheckpointContainer(ctx context.Context, options *runtimeapi.CheckpointContainerRequest) error {\n813: \tr.log(10,\n814: \t\t\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CheckpointContainer\",\n815: \t\t\"options\",\n816: \t\toptions,\n817: \t)\n818: \tif options == nil {\n819: \t\treturn errors.New(\"CheckpointContainer requires non-nil CheckpointRestoreOptions parameter\")\n820: \t}\n821: \tif options.Timeout < 0 {\n822: \t\treturn errors.New(\"CheckpointContainer requires the timeout value to be > 0\")\n823: \t}\n824: \n825: \tctx, cancel := func(ctx context.Context) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\n826: \t\tdefaultTimeout := int64(r.timeout / time.Second)\n827: \t\tif options.Timeout > defaultTimeout {\n828: \t\t\t// The user requested a specific timeout, let's use that if it\n829: \t\t\t// is larger than the CRI default.\n830: \t\t\treturn context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(options.Timeout)*time.Second)\n831: \t\t}\n832: \t\t// If the user requested a timeout less than the\n833: \t\t// CRI default, let's use the CRI default.\n834: \t\toptions.Timeout = defaultTimeout\n835: \t\treturn context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout)\n836: \t}(ctx)\n837: \tdefer cancel()\n838: \n839: \t_, err := r.runtimeClient.CheckpointContainer(\n840: \t\tctx,\n841: \t\toptions,\n842: \t)\n843: \n844: \tif err != nil {\n845: \t\tr.logErr(\n846: \t\t\terr,\n847: \t\t\t\"CheckpointContainer from runtime service failed\",\n848: \t\t\t\"containerID\",\n849: \t\t\toptions.ContainerId,\n850: \t\t)\n851: \t\treturn err\n852: \t}\n853: \tr.log(10,\n854: \t\t\"[RemoteRuntimeService] CheckpointContainer Response\",\n855: \t\t\"containerID\",\n856: \t\toptions.ContainerId,\n857: \t)\n858: \n859: \treturn nil\n860: }\n861: \n862: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) GetContainerEvents(ctx context.Context, containerEventsCh chan *runtimeapi.ContainerEventResponse, connectionEstablishedCallback func(runtimeapi.RuntimeService_GetContainerEventsClient)) error {\n863: \tcontainerEventsStreamingClient, err := r.runtimeClient.GetContainerEvents(ctx, &runtimeapi.GetEventsRequest{})\n864: \tif err != nil {\n865: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"GetContainerEvents failed to get streaming client\")\n866: \t\treturn err\n867: \t}\n868: \n869: \tif connectionEstablishedCallback != nil {\n870: \t\t// The connection is successfully established and we have a streaming client ready for use.\n871: \t\tconnectionEstablishedCallback(containerEventsStreamingClient)\n872: \t}\n873: \n874: \tfor {\n875: \t\tresp, err := containerEventsStreamingClient.Recv()\n876: \t\tif err == io.EOF {\n877: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"container events stream is closed\")\n878: \t\t\treturn err\n879: \t\t}\n880: \t\tif err != nil {\n881: \t\t\tr.logErr(err, \"failed to receive streaming container event\")\n882: \t\t\treturn err\n883: \t\t}\n884: \t\tif resp != nil {\n885: \t\t\tcontainerEventsCh <- resp\n886: \t\t\tr.log(4, \"container event received\", \"resp\", resp)\n887: \t\t}\n888: \t}\n889: }\n890: \n891: // ListMetricDescriptors gets the descriptors for the metrics that will be returned in ListPodSandboxMetrics.\n892: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ListMetricDescriptors(ctx context.Context) ([]*runtimeapi.MetricDescriptor, error) {\n893: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx,
